Our Understanding of Scope
Objective
To deploy a Connected Port Logistics (CPL) web/mobile platform for capturing vessel discharge summaries, digital tokens, and delivery challans, improving efficiency, reducing errors, enabling real-time tracking of coal shipments, and streamlining invoicing via SAP integration.
Scope
Workflow Design: Creating two workflows: vessel discharge and delivery order during sales.
Discharge Summary Trail: Recording discharge details at berth/mid-sea with barge/truck info and photos; Capturing timestamps for truck loading and dumping at designated plots; Store truck images post-loading and at plot arrival.
Digital Token Management: Generating unique digital tokens for trucks with details like truck ID, DO reference, customer ref, and arrival time.
Weighment Slip Records: Capturing weight data from weighbridge slips
Gate Pass Records: Recording gate pass details (truck, weighment info) and sync with SAP.
Delivery Challan Records: Capturing delivery challans including required details and sync with SAP.
SAP Integration: Enabling real-time data exchange between CPL and SAP; Sync delivery orders, gate passes, and challan details.
Executing Development, Implementation, SIT and facilitate UAT
Post Go-live Hypercare Support (1 month)
Key User Training through Train the Trainer approach
Out of Scope
Issues and bugs related to 3rd Party product or services or plugins 
Custom solution developments
Data extraction, cleansing & Reconciliation

Since its inception in 2008, Axestrack has been at the forefront of transforming the logistics and transportation industry through innovative digital solutions. What began as a small-scale operation in Jaipur has evolved into a leading player in India's logistics landscape.
Our growth story is a testament to the power of entrepreneurship. A team of like-minded individuals united to create a company that would redefine the way logistics is done. Today, Axestrack is proud to serve over 250,000+ commercial vehicles across 100+ cities, offering a comprehensive range of digital solutions. (For more about the platform please visit - https://www.axestrack.com/)
Our flagship product, the Axestrack Operating System (commonly known as aOS), leverages cutting-edge AI technology to optimize supply chain operations. By providing real-time data, analytics, and insights, we empower businesses to make informed decisions, improve efficiency, and reduce cost.
Beyond telematics, we offer a comprehensive suite of digital solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. From seamless integration with ERP systems to advanced analytics and AI-driven insights, we empower businesses to build a robust digital thread that drives efficiency and growth. By digitizing information and processes, we help our clients stay ahead of the curve and achieve their long-term goals.
At Axestrack, we believe in partnering with our clients to achieve their strategic objectives. By understanding their unique needs, we tailor our solutions to deliver maximum value. Our commitment to innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction has made us a trusted partner for businesses across India.
Further, we have accomplished ourselves as true partners to some of the marquee clients in the industry. Key mentions are: -

Axestrack with its suite of products (xSwift, Connected Port Logistics, Managed Control Tower, Connected Plant Logistics, etc.) provides state-of-the-art tracking and visibility to shippers and fleet owners.
The unified, single-pane view across all platforms facilitates rapid and informed decision-making. This connected operating system is structured into three key layers: See, Understand & Learn, and Act.
The See layer aggregates data from multiple sources, including third-party GPS/Telematics systems, 3PL providers, ERP/TMS platforms, toll systems, government portals, OEM systems, third-party SIMs, and open APIs. This data is consolidated into a comprehensive data pool via a robust integration layer.
The Understand & Learn layer leverages advanced AI and machine learning capabilities to transform this data lake into a data pool, enabling deeper insights and meaningful analytics.
The Act layer presents this processed data through unified platforms, offering actionable analytics and reports. These insights empower the Managed Control Tower to make real-time interventions, ensuring efficient operations and improved outcomes.

Axestrack has been widely recognized as a top digital solution provider through industry research, awards, and strategic partnerships. Axestrack has received numerous awards and accolades for its innovative digital solutions, including Gartner’s 2024 Market Guide for Fleet Telematics.
Axestrack believes in excellence which led our innovations and product capabilities recognized by multiple analysts and market leaders to solve transportation challenges in the industry.
According to Gartner ‘The fleet telematics market is growing, driven by the increasing demand for efficient fleet management solutions and operational optimization. This growth is catalyzed by widespread telematics adoption, supportive regulatory frameworks, reduced operational costs and enhanced customer satisfaction through real-time visibility on deliveries, driver safety and retention. The integration of AI, ML, and big data analytics is poised to propel the market forward by delivering advanced insights and predictive capabilitiames for fleet management.’

At Axestrack, we are more than a solution provider—we are strategic partner. With over 15 years of experience in logistics and technology, and the management of our own fleet of 200+ vehicles, we leverage our deep industry expertise to proactively analyze key business KPIs and recommend targeted solutions that address operational gaps. As a bootstrapped and profitable organization, we currently manage 2.5 lakh+ fleets and oversee lakhs of trips monthly.
Our in-house Center of Excellence (CoE) designs, develops, and deploys cutting-edge digital solutions tailored to the logistics industry. These solutions support every stage from proof of concept (POC) to full implementation and enhancement. Our unified platform (Connected Port Logistics) seamlessly integrates with any ERP system as a bolt-on solution, enabling faster, more efficient port operations and driving impactful improvements across internal port logistics processes.

ABG Trading faces significant challenges in its port operations, including heavy reliance on manual processes, underutilization of modern technology, and a lack of standardized workflows. These issues result in inefficiencies, errors, and limited visibility across the supply chain. Manual documentation of weighment records, gate passes, and delivery challans creates delays, while the absence of real-time integration with SAP leads to inaccuracies in invoicing and shipment tracking. Additionally, inconsistencies in processes from vessel discharge to the point of sale, coupled with pilferage risks and insufficient compliance mechanisms, further complicate operations.
To address these challenges, Axestrack proposes implementing its flagship solution, Connected Port Logistics (CPL). CPL is a unified digital platform designed to streamline port logistics by automating key touchpoints, digitizing documentation, and integrating real-time data into SAP. The solution includes features such as automated token generation, weighment slip management, and delivery challans, reducing reliance on manual inputs and paperwork. It enhances operational transparency by creating a comprehensive digital audit trail and leverages analytics for process optimization.
CPL will standardize workflows across stages, improve compliance with digital validation checkpoints, and enable real-time tracking of coal shipments. This not only minimizes pilferage risks but also ensures faster and more accurate invoicing. By integrating seamlessly with SAP, CPL provides ABG Trading with a scalable, efficient, and reliable solution, enabling measurable improvements in staff productivity and operational efficiency. This transformation will position ABG Trading as a leader in adopting advanced digital solutions for port operations.
The implementation of Axestrack’s CPL will directly address ABG Trading’s technological, process-related, and people-centric challenges. It will digitize and streamline the entire port-to-point-of-sale workflow, ensure real-time data integration with SAP, and establish a secure, efficient, and auditable system for managing coal shipments. This alignment ensures long-term operational excellence and a sustainable competitive advantage for ABG Trading.

CPL will address the core requirements of ABG Trading by providing the following functionalities:
Vessel Discharge Flow
Capture vessel discharge details, including discharges at berth or mid-sea locations.
Record barge/truck details with timestamps and photographic evidence at key stages:
When trucks load goods at the berth/jetty.
When trucks unload goods at the designated plot.
Allow users to upload discharge summary pictures for better accountability.
Delivery Order Flow
Enable recording and management of delivery orders at the time of sale.
Digital Token Management
Assign a unique digital token for each truck.
Include details such as truck ID, DO reference, customer reference, time of arrival, and other relevant information.
Weighment Slip Management
Record weight data for each truck post-weighment at the bridge.
Generate digital weighment slips that can be printed or emailed.
Gate Pass Management
Capture gate pass details created by the CHA, including truck and weighment data.
Send these details to SAP for further processing.
Delivery Challan Creation
Automatically generate delivery challans based on weighment and token data.
Include key information like DC number, date, vehicle number, transport document, weight, price, and amount.
Send delivery challan details to SAP for seamless integration.
Assigning User as per Segregation of Duties (Roles and Permissions)
Port Operators: Capture and manage tokens, weighment slips, gate pass records, and delivery challans. Access restricted to data specific to their assigned port.
Admin Users: Manage user groups, master data, and transactional data.
Business Users: Access SAP for invoice management and the application for real-time discharge and delivery data.
Executing CPL Development and Deployment
Executing System Integration Testing (SIT) and facilitate User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
Post Go-live Hypercare Support (1 Month)
Key User Training through Train the Trainer approach

Seamless Integration with SAP
Establish a real-time interface for bidirectional data exchange
Delivery order details from SAP to the application.
Gate pass and delivery challan details from the application to SAP.
Build integrations for data to move from Source to end (SAP to CPL; CPL to SAP)
Integrations for internal & external reporting
Total API Integrations – less than 5j

SAP/ Third Party products performance optimization
Data cleansing and data extraction from source system
Issues and bugs related to 3rd Party product or services or plugins
Customization related to 3rd party products
Civil work, Mounting poles, Power supply, LAN connectivity, hardware setup, if any.
Coordination with CHA/Third Party Agent

Backend Development:
Framework: Spring Framework – Scalable and secure modular architecture.
Database: PostgreSQL – Feature-rich, structured data handling with high reliability.
Architecture: Microservices – Independent services to enhance scalability and maintainability.
Mobile Application Development (IOS/Android):
Framework: Flutter – High-performance cross-platform compatibility for iOS and Android.
Programming Language: Dart – Optimized for mobile app efficiency and responsiveness.
Development Methodologies:
Agile Development:
Scrum: Iterative sprints with continuous collaboration and feedback.
Kanban: Visual task management for optimized workflows.
Design-Driven Development:
User-centric design with tools like Figma/Adobe XD and rigorous user testing during prototyping.
DevOps Practices:
Automation: CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins for reliable builds, tests, and deployments.
Monitoring: Integrated logging and performance monitoring
Code Versioning and Collaboration:
Tool: GitLab – Efficient source control with Git Flow branching strategies.
Collaboration: Streamlined team workflows for consistency and quality.
Quality Assurance:
Tool: SonarQube – Continuous code analysis to maintain quality and security standards.
Process: Regular reviews and fixes for bugs and vulnerabilities.
API-First Approach:
API design using Postman for seamless backend and mobile integration.
Performance Monitoring:
Datadog: Real-time backend performance monitoring and infrastructure health.
Firebase Crashlytics: Mobile app crash tracking for a smooth user experience.

Agile-Driven Development:
Iterative updates aligned with client priorities, ensuring adaptability to evolving needs.
User feedback loops integrated into every sprint for tailored solutions.
Microservices Flexibility:
Services can be independently developed, deployed, and scaled.
Fault isolation enhances overall system resilience.
API-First Integration:
APIs designed and tested before development to ensure interoperability.
Enhances modularity between frontend and backend components.
Design-First Philosophy:
Early focus on UX/UI ensures the platform aligns with industry expectations and usability standards.

Maintenance and Updates: Regular software updates.

Customization and Optimization: System customization and performance assessments.
Usability: Provide an intuitive and user-friendly interface for seamless operation.
Compliance and Security: Ensuring regulatory compliance, robust data protection, secure data exchange, user authentication and enhancing data security.
Feedback and Improvement: Collecting user feedback and implementing system enhancements.
Integration Services: Seamless integration with other systems and API support.
Reporting and Analytics: Custom reports and advanced analytics services.
Proactive Monitoring: Regular health checks and alerting services.

Please find the detailed cost estimates in the attached PDF document, which accompanies this proposal. We kindly request you to review the budget details provided for a comprehensive understanding of the financial aspects of the project.

Payment will be on milestone basis
Milestone 1: 100% of implementation cost at the time of project kick-off.
Post Go-live, the billing will be in advance for 1 year on pro-rata basis.
100% payments to be made within 30 days from the date of receipt of original or digitally signed invoice. In case of further delay, overdue charges of 1.5% per month will be charged
Billing validation/certification to be made within 7 days from the date of provisional bill submission.
All the prices mentioned in proposal are in rupees and exclusive of GST. Taxes as per applicable
Minimum contract period is for 3 Years post Go-Live.

A global leader in construction materials manufacturing faced significant challenges in ensuring vehicle safety compliance during loading operations at its production facilities. The company operates in over 50 countries and is known for producing cement, aggregates, and ready-mix concrete.
A leading conglomerate in the food and agricultural sector specializing in palm oil production faced challenges in maintaining oil quality during transit, ETA management, automated sequencing and optimizing in-plant operations
A leading cement manufacturer, faced challenges in vehicle tracking, route deviations, detention, and pilferage across their logistics operations. These issues were further compounded by manual document checks, inefficient trip management, and high operational costs.

CPL is supported through monthly, quarterly, and annual updates to maintain optimal performance and meet evolving needs.
Best Practices for Support and Maintenance:
Scheduled Updates:
Monthly: Bug fixes, performance optimizations, and minor enhancements.
Quarterly: Feature releases, system audits, and design updates.
Yearly: Comprehensive performance reviews, architectural upgrades, and long-term scalability planning.
Proactive Monitoring:
Real-time monitoring with Datadog and Firebase Crashlytics to detect and address issues.
Alerts and logs are analyzed regularly to ensure system health.
Continuous Quality Assurance:
Code analysis with SonarQube to uphold code quality and security.
Automated testing pipelines to detect issues before deployment.
Collaboration and Feedback
Regular client check-ins to align on feature requests and enhancements.
Feedback incorporated into the Agile workflow for iterative improvement.
Scalability and Performance Management:
Ongoing optimization of microservices for handling increased loads.
Database performance tuning and regular indexing with PostgreSQL.
Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:
Maintain up-to-date technical documentation for APIs, workflows, and troubleshooting.
Provide training sessions or resources for client teams as needed.
Incident Management:
Implement a structured escalation process for resolving critical issues swiftly.
Root cause analysis for incidents to prevent recurrence.
With this technical robustness and structured methodology, CPL ensures seamless port operations and long-term client satisfaction.

Axestrack has three layered approaches to Governance
Steering committee and value board: This layer will have the executive sponsor for the project with one-to-one mapping with the ABG Trading Sponsors level management. The team will meet monthly/quarterly to validate and monitor the project progress and take strategic decisions. This layer is the highest level of escalation,
Enagament & project management: This layer provides the project level monitoring and management. They will deal with the day-to-day operation issues and escalations. This layer will be responsible for the project success and excellence.
Project operations & transformation: This layer involves all the members of execution team including Technical SPOCs, functional SPOCs and SMEs. These will interact with the client project team on daily basis and work for the success of the project.

Civil work, Mounting poles, Power supply, LAN connectivity, hardware setup, if any, are out of scope for the project
ABG Trading will provide the timely approvals and sign-off for the work products (Digital Layer) delivered as part of project.
Coordination with CHA/Third Party Agent for data inputs in CPL digital layer will be done by ABG Trading team.
Any new requirements beyond the scope of this proposal will be considered as change request for which separate effort & cost to be estimated.
Data extraction, cleansing, validation & reconciliation from SAP will be done by ABG Trading business users.
Issues and bugs related to 3rd Party product or services or plugins will be out of scope for this project.
All project deliverables will be in English.
Local language support or any translation requirement to be supported by ABG Trading.
The Network, security, IT and other infrastructure for this project to be owned by ABG Trading team. Axestrack’s responsibility is only to provide recommendation related to these areas.
It will be SaaS deployment solution. On-Premise network configuration will be out of scope.
Any hardware requirement will be charged separately.
All communication with SAP will be through API Integration
Checkpoints/Stages data inputs & validation will be done by ground operation team (ABG Trading / CHA) by dynamic checklist
ABG Trading team will be providing instruction and sign-off after each testing stage (i.e SIT & UAT).
Axestrack will be dependent on ABG Trading for any delay due to major deviation, weather/environmental conditions, new requirements and non-availability of ABG Trading SPOCs (users & project team) resulting in project timeline delay will not be Axestrack responsibility.
Train the trainer approach will be followed for the user training
ABG Trading need to provide all the required approvals and passes to the Axestrack Onsite team (if required during project implementation) at Port Terminal. Also, ABG Trading will be responsible to provide Safety Training & PPE’s to Axestrack’s team.

Axestrack’s product roadmap has been built keeping current and future needs of the industry in scope. The roadmap establishes our commitment to the innovations and capabilities which we co-develop with client to ensure these developments are relevant to the needs of the clients.
The intent behind this vision is simple. We understand that “connectivity” is the way for future and hence the core of any solution must embrace the connected part and should support the mechanisms which promotes connectivity.
We understand that while vision is clear, we also need to keep the structural elements flexible and modular. Hence, the design of our architecture and roadmap has been envisioned to be ‘modular and agile’.

content:
Hardware Warranty & AMC
Terms and Conditions
Standard Warranty Coverage
All installed hardware will carry a Standard One-Year OEM Warranty from the date the equipment is unpacked and the seal is broken, marking its readiness for installation and operational use. As per OEM policy, the hardware warranty period begins once the equipment is unpacked and the seal is broken, as it is then considered commissioned for operational use. Accordingly, we will consider the warranty start date from the point when the hardware is opened from its original packaging and made ready for installation. This includes coverage for hardware malfunction, firmware issues, and initial configuration defects, provided the equipment has been used under prescribed operating conditions.
Inclusions Under Standard Warranty
The following are covered:
Hardware faults due to manufacturing defects
Firmware issues or embedded software malfunctions
Initial configuration or calibration problems
Functional issues arising under normal operating environments
Exclusions from Warranty
The standard warranty will not cover:
Physical damage, dents, or broken components
Burnt devices due to electrical surges or short circuits
Waterlogging, corrosion, or environmental damage
Vandalism, unauthorized repairs, or tampering with device internals
These are standard OEM warranty terms and cannot be altered.
Post-Warranty Support via AMC
After the one-year warranty period, support will only be provided under an Annual Maintenance Contract (AMC). The AMC scope and pricing will be governed by the terms outlined in the final commercial agreement.
AMC Coverage
The AMC will include:
Functional support and repair for eligible hardware components
Preventive maintenance checks (if opted)
Firmware updates and remote diagnostics (where applicable)
AMC Exclusions
AMC will not include the following:
Replacement due to physical/environmental damage
Reinstallation caused by infrastructure changes (e.g., shifting or rewiring)
Repairs of third-party equipment not supplied by Axestrack
Component Availability & Obsolescence
In case any component becomes obsolete during AMC, the client will be informed. Equivalent or upgraded components (if available) will be proposed separately at additional cost.
Support SLAs
Response and resolution times will follow the SLA guidelines mutually agreed in the commercial contract, depending on equipment type and issue category.
Site Access & Client Obligations
Clients must provide safe, stable power, proper network (if applicable), and access for maintenance activities. Inappropriate environmental or usage conditions may void warranty claims.
Out-of-Scope Repairs (Post-Warranty/Without AMC)
Any repair requests outside warranty/AMC will be handled on a chargeable basis covering diagnosis, spares, labor, and travel, as applicable.

Axestrack stands as a pioneer in transforming the logistics industry with innovative solutions that redefine fleet management. With over 15 years of expertise and only Indian company ever to be recognized in Gartner’s 2024 Market Guide for Fleet Telematics Solutions, we are a trusted name for businesses seeking smarter, safer, and more efficient operations.
At the core of our offerings is the Axestrack Operating System (aOS), a next-generation AI platform designed to go beyond traditional tracking. Built on advanced analytics, intelligent automation, and real-time insights, aOS delivers comprehensive solutions to optimize fleet productivity, reduce TAT, providing end-to-end visibility and control for inbound, in-plant, outbound and port operations and improve operational efficiency. (For more about the platform please visit - https://www.axestrack.com/)
Axestrack is more than just a technology provider; we are a partner in driving innovation. Our solutions for Gate Operations (GOS), Yard Management, Logistics platform and many more brings together real-time visibility, actionable insights, and intelligent control, enabling businesses to predict, prevent, and resolve challenges before they escalate.
Our impact is far-reaching, with aOS managing over 250,000 commercial vehicles globally, for 100+ clients and enabling smarter logistics across diverse industries like Cement, Automobile, FMCG, E-commerce, Chemical, etc. Whether it’s fleet owners, consignors, plants, or port authorities, we deliver tailored solutions that drive efficiency and compliance.
Recognized globally and trusted locally, Axestrack is committed to shaping the future of logistics. We are investing in advanced telematics, IoT integrations, and cloud-based control towers to ensure our clients stay ahead in an ever-evolving landscape.
With Axestrack, every journey becomes smarter, safer, and future-ready.
Few of our successful customers / references.

Axestrack with its suite of products (xSwift, Connected Port Logistics, Gen AI Based Managed Control Tower, Connected Port Logistics, etc.) provides state-of-the-art tracking and visibility to shippers and fleet owners.
The unified, single-pane view across all platforms facilitates rapid and informed decision-making. This connected operating system is structured into three key layers: See, Understand & Learn, and Act.
The See layer aggregates data from multiple sources, including third-party GPS/Telematics systems, 3PL providers, TOS/ERP/TMS platforms, toll systems, government portals, OEM systems, third-party SIMs, and open APIs. This data is consolidated into a comprehensive data pool via a robust integration layer.
The Understand & Learn layer leverages advanced AI and machine learning capabilities to transform this data lake into a data pool, enabling deeper insights and meaningful analytics.
The Act layer presents this processed data through unified platforms, offering actionable analytics and reports. These insights empower the Managed Control Tower to make real-time interventions, ensuring efficient operations and improved outcomes.

Axestrack has been widely recognized as a top digital solution provider through industry research, awards, and strategic partnerships. Axestrack has received numerous awards and accolades for its innovative digital solutions, including Gartner’s 2024 Market Guide for Fleet Telematics.
Axestrack believes in excellence which led our innovations and product capabilities recognized by multiple analysts and market leaders to solve transportation challenges in the industry.
According to Gartner ‘The fleet telematics market is growing, driven by the increasing demand for efficient fleet management solutions and operational optimization. This growth is catalyzed by widespread telematics adoption, supportive regulatory frameworks, reduced operational costs and enhanced customer satisfaction through real-time visibility on deliveries, driver safety and retention. The integration of AI, ML, and big data analytics is poised to propel the market forward by delivering advanced insights and predictive capabilities for fleet management.’

At Axestrack, we go beyond being a solution provider—we are a strategic partner in port and plant digitalization. With over 15 years of expertise in logistics technology and deep industry insights, we deliver scalable, future-ready solutions that optimize port operations. Our solutions are built for high-volume, mission-critical environments managing 30+ enterprise clients.
Our in-house Center of Excellence (CoE) specializes in designing, developing, and deploying XSWIFT—a unified, cloud-native platform that integrates seamlessly with any ERP, TMS, WMS. From Proof of Concept (PoC) to full-scale deployment, XSWIFT enhances logistics movement tracking and automation, gate management, vehicle tracking, and real-time operational visibility.
As a bootstrapped, profitable organization, Axestrack ensures long-term stability, innovation, and continuous improvement in port logistics, enabling clients to achieve higher efficiency, reduced turnaround times, and seamless cargo movement within port ecosystems.

Adani Kattupalli Port handles a large and diverse mix of containerized, liquid, and loose cargo. Current gate operations are semi-manual, with multiple checkpoints across Main Gate, New/Old Gate Complexes, Parking Yard, CFS, and Liquid Cargo gates. This leads to delays, increased turnaround time (TAT), security risks, and lack of real-time visibility.
To support Adani’s growth and customer service commitments, a future-ready RFID and IoT-based Gate Operation System (GOS) is needed. The system must:
Automate vehicle validation and gate operations
Integrate seamlessly with ITUP, IPOS, and Navis N4 for container and cargo workflows
Provide real-time dashboards, audit logs, and MIS for end-to-end trip visibility
Enhance safety, compliance, and operational efficiency

Manual dependency at multiple gates increases TAT and causes congestion.
Limited integration between gate operations and TOS/booking applications results in data duplication and errors.
Lack of real-time visibility of truck movement across Export, Import, CFS, and Liquid flows creates planning inefficiencies.
Ineffective threshold control allows excess trucks inside the terminal, leading to yard congestion.
Inconsistent compliance checks (PUC, insurance, vehicle fitness) create security and regulatory risks.
Limited reporting & analytics restrict management’s ability to track performance and optimize cost.

The Connected Port Logistics (CPoL) solution is aligned with Adani’s digital transformation objectives by delivering a comprehensive, integrated gate automation platform that ties hardware, software, and enterprise applications into a seamless ecosystem.
End-to-End Process Coverage
Deployment of RFID readers, ANPR cameras, boom barriers, LED displays, and CCTV across all operational gates: Main Gate, New Gate Complex, Old Gate Complex, Parking Yard, CFS Gate, and Liquid Pass Gate.
Each gate is mapped in the Gate Operation System (GOS) with Location ID, Lane ID, Device IDs, and IP mapping to ensure reliable gate-level visibility.
Seamless System Integration
ITUP ↔ GOS (JSON APIs): Master synchronization of Port Entry Permits (PEP), Vehicle–RFID mapping, and GP indent types.
Hardware readiness: RFID, Camera, LED Display, lane controllers, and boom barriers connected to GOS.
GOS ↔ IPOS-NC (JSON APIs): Real-time gate validation (tag number, lane ID, GP type).
GOS ↔ Navis N4 (XML APIs): Import/Export container milestones posted as Gate Events, CT Gate Events, and RTG Events.
RDT Server (TCP Port 3590): Integration for manual boom-up requests with operator ID and reason logging.
mParivahan (REST APIs): Vehicle compliance checks for PUC, fitness, and insurance before gate clearance.
POS/UPI systems: Gate/parking fee validation integrated into GOS prior to boom opening.
Dashboards: Consolidation of all gate events, parking occupancy, congestion thresholds, and exception alerts.
SIT/UAT cycles: Validation of interfaces under live traffic conditions.
Real-Time Visibility & Control
A centralized dashboard provides lane-wise visibility, transaction timestamps, queue length, parking yard occupancy, and container milestones.
Configurable threshold controls (per gate/per lane) prevent congestion by restricting boom opening once terminal occupancy exceeds pre-defined limits.
Telemetry feeds from RFID readers, boom barriers, and cameras enable proactive maintenance and SLA monitoring.
Efficiency & Security
Performance: RFID/ANPR-based authentication ensures gate clearance in <5 seconds per vehicle
Security: End-to-end encryption, token-based API authentication, and RBAC for RDT/manual overrides.
Reliability: Redundant readers, dual power supply (UPS-backed), and automated retry with event idempotency guarantee uptime and data integrity.
Auditability: Every transaction is logged with vehicle number, tag ID, lane ID, timestamp, decision, and operator action (if applicable).
Value Creation
Faster truck turnaround: Improved throughput across Import, Export, CFS, and Liquid flows.
Reduced manual interventions: Automated validations and integrated milestone logging cut down dependency on manual checks.
Compliance assurance: Integration with mParivahan and audit trails minimize regulatory risk.
Future-proof scalability: Modular, API-first architecture scales to 2× current traffic volume without performance degradation.

The proposed RFID-based Gate Operation System (GOS), integrated with Connected Port Logistics, will deliver:
Gate Automation
Vehicle authentication using RFID readers and optional ANPR cameras.
Automated boom barrier and traffic light operation at all defined gates.
LED displays for driver guidance at entry/exit lanes.
Process Coverage
Export & Import flows: Main Gate, Parking Yard, New Gate, Old Gate.
CFS operations: Gate In/Gate Out transactions with IPOS sync.
Liquid Cargo operations: Gate In/Gate Out validations integrated with IPOS Liquid module.
System Features
Configurable threshold control to prevent excess trucks entering terminals.
Automatic alerts for overstays, wrong vehicle entry, and exception handling.
Real-time dashboards for gate operators, control tower, and management.
MIS and analytics: TAT, gate logs, parking occupancy, cargo type-wise flow.
Integration Scope
ITUP: Vehicle, RFID Tag, and GP Indent sync.
IPOS: Gate validations for Dry Cargo, CFS, and Liquid Cargo.
Navis N4: Container gate entry/exit milestone updates.
RDT Server: Manual boom-up requests (TCP).
mParivahan: Vehicle compliance validation (fitness, insurance, PUC).
POS/UPI systems: Parking/gate fee collections.
Civil & Electrical Works Scope
Execution of minor civil works such as pole and boom barrier foundation, cable trenching from pole to junction box, and preparation for LAN/Wi-Fi connectivity.
Installation of enclosures, junction boxes, and associated electrical terminations at designated gate locations.
Power and LAN/Wi-Fi connectivity up to the termination end point (junction box) shall be provided and made ready by Adani Port, ensuring network and power availability at designated locations. From the junction box onward, all internal cabling and connections for pole-mounted devices will be executed by Axestrack.
All works will be executed through a client-approved or recommended agency/vendor, adhering to Adani’s quality and safety standards.
Execution Methodology:
Activities will follow a client-defined quality checklist and be verified jointly during site readiness sign-off.
The executing vendor shall provide warranty/guarantee documentation for completed works.
Hardware installation and commissioning will be carried out by Axestrack post completion of civil and electrical readiness.

Hardware Supply & Installation
RFID readers, boom barriers, ANPR cameras, Handheld RFID Readers, Industrial Grade Tablets, LED displays, CCTV.
Software & Integration
Gate Operation System (GOS) application with APIs for ITUP, IPOS, Navis N4, and other defined systems.
Centralized dashboard with real-time monitoring and analytics.
Secure APIs with HTTPS and token-based authentication.
System performance optimized to <5 seconds validation per vehicle.
Infrastructure & Support
System configuration, testing, UAT, commissioning, and warranty.
Server setup requirements defined for UAT, Pre-Prod, and Production environments.
Comprehensive training (Train-The-Trainer) for Adani staff.
Documentation: user guides, technical manuals, SOPs.

The following activities are excluded from the proposed scope:
Engaging in Customs or other regulatory bodies for compliance activities not related to automation.
RFID Tags placement and mapping in system will done by Adani Port team.
Trade documentation initiatives beyond gate automation workflows.
Wagon-level container mapping, siding operations, or rake utilization optimization.
Customizations to ERP/TOS systems (ITUP, IPOS, and Navis N4) or third-party software outside defined API integrations. - The application APIs are compatible with ITUP, IPOS, and Navis N4 systems as per the defined integration scope.
ERP modules outside logistics scope (Finance, HR, Procurement, etc.).
Custom TOS modifications not related to vehicle movement/container movement/trip lifecycle..
Major civil construction, large-scale trenching, structural modification, or power feeder extension beyond defined gate automation areas shall remain outside Axestrack’s scope.
Manual interventions beyond defined automation (e.g., manual weighbridge ops, physical checks).
Hardware not supplied or supported under this project.
Coordination with third-party vendors/agents for systems not in the defined integration scope. Axestrack will liaise and coordinate with the ITUP, IPOS, and Navis teams of Adani Ports to ensure successful integration and smooth implementation of the project.
Data cleansing/migration from legacy or manual records.

The proposed Connected Port Logistics (CPoL) – RFID Gate Automation System (GOS) at Adani Kattupalli will deliver a comprehensive, integrated, and automated gate management platform that streamlines entry/exit operations for Import, Export, CFS, Dry Cargo and Liquid Cargo flows.
The solution leverages RFID readers, ANPR cameras, boom barriers, LED displays, Kiosk Printer, Handheld RFID Readers, Industrial grade Tablet and CCTV (Smart Cameras), combined with seamless API integration with ITUP, IPOS, and Navis N4, ensuring secure, efficient, and transparent cargo movement across the port.
Note - Remote boom barrier operation through the Web/tablet interface is part of the proposed solution.

General Flow:
Main Gate In → Parking Yard → New Gate In → Old Gate In → Stack Yard (Pickup) → Old Gate Out → New Gate Out → Main Gate Out
Validation points: Main Gate In, Parking Yard (Plaza), New Gate In, Old Gate In, Old Gate Out, New Gate Out, Main Gate Out.
Navis N4 Integration: Import milestone logged when container is picked up at yard. We plan to integrate with Navis N4 via API to capture container movement events (truck loading/unloading at the yard). This milestone will then be validated at the Old Gate Complex, enabling a single-window view of container status across systems. If required, this integration-based milestone can be excluded.
Control features: Threshold truck entry at New Gate & Old Gate and can be configured as per Adani’s requirements; compliance checks before Main Gate Out.
Outcome: Secure pickup validation, reduced dwell time, real-time trip visibility.

General Flow:
Main Gate In → Parking Yard → New Gate In → Old Gate In → Stack Yard (Drop) → Old Gate Out → New Gate Out → Main Gate Out
Validation points: Same as Import (all gates).
Navis N4 Integration: Export milestone logged when container is dropped at yard. For export flow, we plan to integrate with Navis N4 via API to capture the milestone when the container is dropped at the yard (truck unloading event). This event will be validated at the Old Gate Complex, ensuring a unified single-window view of export container status. If required, this milestone can be excluded from integration.
Control features: Parking Yard monitoring to avoid premature entry, congestion control at New Gate.
Outcome: Faster export clearance, automated milestone logging, minimized manual checks.

Modified Flow:
Main Gate In → CFS Gate In → CFS Gate Out → Main Gate Out
Additional Gate Checks:
CFS Gate In/Out with RFID readers and IPOS/ ITPU sync for loose cargo validation.
Integration: CFS transactions logged in IPOS, milestones tracked separately from containerized cargo.
Control features: Traffic lights and boom barrier control for wrong vehicle exit.
Outcome: Transparent CFS cargo handling, accurate in/out logging, improved yard visibility.

Simplified Flow:
Main Gate In → Liquid Pass Gate In → Unload/Load (Manual) → Liquid Pass Gate Out → Main Gate Out
Gate Checks:
RFID validation at Main Gate In, Liquid Pass In/Out (3 Bidirectional Gates), Unload/Load point via Handheld RFID Readers (Manual input) and Main Gate Out.
Integration: IPOS and ITUP Liquid Cargo sync for tanker unloading milestones.
Compliance: mParivahan checks (PUC, insurance, fitness) enforced before unloading.
Outcome: Fast tanker turnaround, minimized congestion, automated milestone reporting for safety-critical cargo.

Note - Integrations will be implemented as per the defined project scope. Any major change or additional integration effort beyond the agreed scope will be managed through a formal Change Request (CR) process, to be mutually discussed and approved. All minor adjustments during requirement gathering will remain within scope.

Across all four flows, the Connected Port Logistics (CPoL) solution ensures:
Standardized gate automation with RFID/ANPR validation at each milestone. - “Standardized gate automation” refers to a unified CPoL platform ensuring consistent validation, process flow, and audit logging across all gates. Each in/out point follows the same operational logic with integrated RFID/ANPR checks, compliance validations, and real-time visibility for centralized control and reporting.
System integrations (ITUP, IPOS, Navis N4, mParivahan, RDT) across cargo types.
Configurable thresholds & controls to manage congestion dynamically.
Dashboards & MIS to track Import, Export, CFS, and Liquid flows separately, while maintaining a unified control tower view.

Note – As suggested, the Liquid Pass In and Liquid Pass Out gates will be combined into a three bidirectional gate. This update has been reflected accordingly in the design. Please refer to the hardware list for the revised BOQ.

Post Implementation key services include:
Technical Support: 24/7 help desk and remote diagnostics.
Maintenance and Updates: Regular software updates.
Data Management: Continuous data monitoring and analytics.
Training and Support: User training sessions and resource materials.
Usability: Provide an intuitive and user-friendly interface for seamless operation.
Feedback and Improvement: Collecting user feedback and implementing system enhancements.
Reporting and Analytics: Custom reports and advanced analytics services.
Proactive Monitoring: Regular health checks and alerting services.

Axestrack has three layered approaches to Governance
Steering committee and value board: This layer will have the executive sponsor for the project with one-to-one mapping with the Adani Port’s Sponsors level management. The team will meet monthly/quarterly to validate and monitor the project progress and take strategic decisions. This layer is the highest level of escalation,
Enagament & project management: This layer provides the project level monitoring and management. They will deal with the day-to-day operation issues and escalations. This layer will be responsible for the project success and excellence.
Project operations & transformation: This layer involves all the members of execution team including Technical SPOCs, functional SPOCs and SMEs. These will interact with the client project team on daily basis and work for the success of the project.

The Executive Dashboard provides a comprehensive, high-level view of Port operations in real time. It consolidates KPIs such as turnaround time (TAT), gate-in/out counts, weighbridge performance, rake status, ITV utilization, and alerts into one screen. This enables senior management to track overall efficiency, spot bottlenecks instantly, and take informed decisions without depending on multiple reports.

Axestrack’s product roadmap has been built keeping current and future needs of the industry in scope. The roadmap establishes our commitment to the innovations and capabilities which we co-develop with client to ensure these developments are relevant to the needs of the clients.
The intent behind this vision is simple. We understand that “connectivity” is the way for future and hence the core of any solution must embrace the connected part and should support the mechanisms which promotes connectivity.
We understand that while vision is clear, we also need to keep the structural elements flexible and modular. Hence, the design of our architecture and roadmap has been envisioned to be ‘modular and agile’.

Axestrack's unique advantage over competitors is our Multi Tracking mode, providing nearly 100% trip visibility irrespective of any failures
In the event that GPS fails, Axestrack's auto fail safe engine will automatically switch to Driver SIM. If, driver SIM also fails, the system will switch to Conductor SIM tracking, utilizing both the driver's and conductor's SIM cards. This ensures that the client maintains close to 100% visibility of their vehicle throughout the entire trip, providing continuous tracking and ensuring that the client never loses track of their vehicle.

Since its inception in 2008, Axestrack has been at the forefront of transforming the logistics and transportation industry through innovative digital solutions. What began as a small-scale operation in Jaipur has evolved into a leading player in India's logistics landscape.
Our growth story is a testament to the power of entrepreneurship. A team of like-minded individuals united to create a company that would redefine the way logistics is done. Today, Axestrack is proud to serve over 250,000+ commercial vehicles across 100+ cities, offering a comprehensive range of digital solutions. (For more about the platform please visit - https://www.axestrack.com/)
Our flagship product, the Axestrack Operating System (commonly known as aOS), leverages cutting-edge AI technology to optimize supply chain operations. By providing real-time data, analytics, and insights, we empower businesses to make informed decisions, improve efficiency, and reduce cost.
Beyond telematics, we offer a comprehensive suite of digital solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. From seamless integration with ERP systems to advanced analytics and AI-driven insights, we empower businesses to build a robust digital thread that drives efficiency and growth. By digitizing information and processes, we help our clients stay ahead of the curve and achieve their long-term goals.
At Axestrack, we believe in partnering with our clients to achieve their strategic objectives. By understanding their unique needs, we tailor our solutions to deliver maximum value. Our commitment to innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction has made us a trusted partner for businesses across India.
Further, we have accomplished ourselves as true partners to some of the marquee clients in the industry. Key mentions are: -

Axestrack with its suite of products (xSwift, Connected Plant Logistics, Managed Control Tower, Connected Port Logistics, etc.) provides state-of-the-art tracking and visibility to shippers and fleet owners.
The unified, single-pane view across all platforms facilitates rapid and informed decision-making. This connected operating system is structured into three key layers: See, Understand & Learn, and Act.
The See layer aggregates data from multiple sources, including third-party GPS/Telematics systems, 3PL providers, ERP/TMS platforms, toll systems, government portals, OEM systems, third-party SIMs, and open APIs. This data is consolidated into a comprehensive data pool via a robust integration layer.
The Understand & Learn layer leverages advanced AI and machine learning capabilities to transform this data lake into a data pool, enabling deeper insights and meaningful analytics.
The Act layer presents this processed data through unified platforms, offering actionable analytics and reports. These insights empower the Managed Control Tower to make real-time interventions, ensuring efficient operations and improved outcomes.

Axestrack has been widely recognized as a top digital solution provider through industry research, awards, and strategic partnerships. Axestrack has received numerous awards and accolades for its innovative digital solutions, including Gartner’s 2024 Market Guide for Fleet Telematics.
Axestrack believes in excellence which led our innovations and product capabilities recognized by multiple analysts and market leaders to solve transportation challenges in the industry.
According to Gartner ‘The fleet telematics market is growing, driven by the increasing demand for efficient fleet management solutions and operational optimization. This growth is catalyzed by widespread telematics adoption, supportive regulatory frameworks, reduced operational costs and enhanced customer satisfaction through real-time visibility on deliveries, driver safety and retention. The integration of AI, ML, and big data analytics is poised to propel the market forward by delivering advanced insights and predictive capabilities for fleet management.’

At Axestrack, we are more than a solution provider—we are strategic partner. With over 15 years of experience in logistics and technology, and the management of our own fleet of 200+ vehicles, we leverage our deep industry expertise to proactively analyze key business KPIs and recommend targeted solutions that address operational gaps. As a bootstrapped and profitable organization, we currently manage 2.5 lakh+ fleets and oversee lakhs of trips monthly.
Our in-house Center of Excellence (CoE) designs, develops, and deploys cutting-edge digital solutions tailored to the logistics industry. These solutions support every stage from proof of concept (POC) to full implementation and enhancement. Our unified platform (Connected Plant Logistics) seamlessly integrates with any ERP system as a bolt-on solution, enabling faster, more efficient plant operations and driving impactful improvements across internal plant logistics processes.

AMNS operates an integrated steel plant at Hazira, Surat, which is currently undergoing a major capacity expansion aimed at nearly doubling its production capacity. Earlier, all finished goods dispatches were centrally managed through a common marshalling yard, enabling controlled vehicle movement and centralized traffic coordination. As part of the expansion initiative, the central marshalling yard has been dismantled, and dispatch operations have been decentralized. Individual production units such as CRM-1, CRM-2, HRM, and other departments now manage dispatches independently through dedicated warehouses located adjacent to their manufacturing facilities.
This decentralization has resulted in a substantial increase in internal vehicular traffic without any corresponding enhancement in road infrastructure. The impact is most pronounced in the CRM-1 zone, which experiences severe congestion during peak hours—particularly in the evening during shift changes when employee movement overlaps with commercial vehicle traffic. In addition to increased traffic volume, CRM-1 also faces challenges related to poor vehicle discipline at loading points. Incoming trucks often park in unauthorized areas, including active traffic lanes, further aggravating congestion and creating safety risks. Moreover, vehicles are frequently required to reverse into loading bays, increasing the likelihood of accidents due to blind spots and limited maneuvering space. In the recent past, multiple near-miss incidents have been reported in this area, highlighting the urgent need for improved traffic control, parking discipline, and safer loading practices within the plant.

Uncontrolled increase in internal vehicular traffic due to decentralized dispatch operations without corresponding road infrastructure upgrades
Severe congestion in critical zones (especially CRM-1) during peak hours caused by the overlap of employee movement and commercial vehicle dispatches
Unauthorized parking of trucks near loading areas, including on active traffic lanes, leading to traffic bottlenecks and safety risks
Unsafe vehicle maneuvering practices, particularly reversing into loading bays, increasing blind-spot and collision hazards
Rising safety exposure, evidenced by multiple near-miss incidents involving vehicles and personnel in recent months
Lack of centralized traffic monitoring and enforcement, resulting in poor vehicle discipline and inconsistent adherence to safety protocols

To address the growing internal traffic congestion and safety risks at the AMNS Hazira plant, Axestrack has conducted a detailed on-ground assessment by visiting the facility and engaging with key stakeholders across operations and logistics. Based on this assessment, Axestrack proposes the deployment of its Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) platform, a comprehensive, AI-powered solution designed to optimize internal traffic flow, enhance safety, and bring centralized visibility across decentralized dispatch operations.
The CPL platform leverages a smart grid of hardware devices, intelligent traffic logic, and advanced analytics to regulate vehicle movement inside the plant in real time. By dynamically managing traffic at critical junctions, loading bays, and high-risk zones such as CRM-1, the platform ensures smoother vehicle flow, enforces discipline, minimizes unsafe maneuvers, and significantly reduces safety hazards. The solution not only addresses current operational challenges but also creates a data-driven foundation for continuous improvement in plant logistics and traffic planning.
Illustrative Image:

CPL deploys intelligent traffic management units at critical tri-junctions and intersections inside the steel plant, especially near CRM-1 and dispatch corridors. Each junction is equipped with smart cameras, traffic controllers, and AI-based decision logic that continuously monitors vehicle movement across all incoming and outgoing lanes.
The system dynamically regulates traffic by prioritizing lanes based on real-time conditions such as peak shift hours, congestion levels, vehicle type (commercial vs employee transport), and vehicle reversal at loading bay. Unlike fixed traffic signals, CPL adapts signal timing and lane permissions automatically based on live data.
Advantages
Smoothens traffic flow during peak shift change hours
Prevents congestion spillover across plant.
Reduces manual traffic intervention by security personnel
Improves overall throughput without modifying road infrastructure

CPL enforces controlled entry into sensitive or high-traffic zones such as CRM-1 by dynamically limiting the number of vehicles allowed at any given time. Cameras and access logic track how many vehicles are already present and automatically permit or restrict new entries via traffic regulations based on predefined safety and capacity thresholds.
This ensures that traffic inside constrained areas remain within manageable limits and prevents sudden surges caused by uncoordinated vehicular movement.
Advantages
Eliminates overloading of narrow or high-risk internal roads
Maintains safe vehicle density in CRM-1 and similar zones
Reduces gridlocks caused by simultaneous vehicle arrivals
Brings discipline and predictability to vehicular movements

At loading bays, CPL deploys smart cameras with AI-based human and vehicle detection, integrated with PA systems. When a vehicle enters reverse mode for loading, the system continuously scans blind spots and the surrounding area for any manpower movement or obstruction.
If a safety risk is detected, the system immediately provides audio alerts to the driver and notifies nearby personnel, guiding the driver to stop or adjust movement until the area is clear.
Advantages
Significantly reduces blind-spot related accidents and near-misses
Protects shop-floor personnel during reversing operations
Provides real-time guidance instead of post-incident reporting

CPL continuously monitors internal plant lanes using smart cameras installed at fixed intervals. The AI engine identifies vehicles parked in unauthorized areas, or active traffic lanes and instantly flags these violations.
Real-time alerts are pushed to the safety and operations teams, enabling immediate corrective action before congestion or safety incidents escalate.
Advantages
Prevents blockage of main traffic corridors
Improves vehicle discipline near loading and waiting areas
Enables faster response by safety and security teams
Reduces secondary congestion caused by illegal parking

All traffic data from junctions, loading bays, and plant roads is consolidated into a centralized CPL dashboard. Safety and logistics teams get real-time visibility into vehicle movement, congestion hotspots, violations, and abnormal patterns.
This allows proactive traffic control instead of reactive incident handling.
Advantages
Single source of truth for plant traffic operations
Faster incident detection and resolution
Improved coordination between logistics, safety, and operations teams
Enables informed decision-making during peak or emergency situations

CPL records detailed movement data such as vehicle entry/exit time, dwell time at particular stretch, route taken, and congestion patterns. This historical data is analysed to identify recurring bottlenecks, unsafe zones, and peak traffic trends.
Over time, this enables AMNS to fine-tune dispatch schedules, traffic rules, and infrastructure planning.
Advantages
Data-driven optimization of dispatch and traffic planning
Continuous improvement of safety inside the plant
Supports future capacity expansion planning
Moves traffic management from reactive to predictive

Deployment and configuration of smart devices and sensors
Building a network of interconnected hardware systems
Digital twin creation to enable operational visibility

AMNS will provide the timely approvals and sign-off for the work products (Digital Layer) delivered as part of project.
We assume all vehicles to be equipped with fast tag/RFID cards for vehicle counting and traffic regulation.
The above commercials do not include the Civil and IT infrastructure. AMNS has to provide the same.
Any new requirements beyond the scope of this proposal will be considered as change request for which separate effort & cost to be estimated.
Issues and bugs related to 3rd Party product or services or plugins will be out of scope for this project.
All project deliverables will be in English.
The Network, security, IT and other infrastructure for this project to be owned by AMNS team. Axestrack’s responsibility is only to provide recommendation related to these areas.
It will be SaaS deployment solution. On-Premise network configuration will be out of scope.
Any hardware requirement will be charged separately.
AMNS team will be providing instruction and sign-off after each testing stage (i.e SIT & UAT).
Train the trainer approach will be followed for the user training
AMNS need to provide all the required approvals and passes to the Axestrack Onsite team (if required during project implementation) at Plant.

Axestrack’s product roadmap has been built keeping current and future needs of the industry in scope. The roadmap establishes our commitment to the innovations and capabilities which we co-develop with client to ensure these developments are relevant to the needs of the clients.
The intent behind this vision is simple. We understand that “connectivity” is the way for future and hence the core of any solution must embrace the connected part and should support the mechanisms which promotes connectivity.
We understand that while vision is clear, we also need to keep the structural elements flexible and modular. Hence, the design of our architecture and roadmap has been envisioned to be ‘modular and agile’.

the core business being steelmaking. The Nippon Steel Group has manufacturing facilities in  over 15 countries and is 
committed to provide high -performance and high -quality steel products to its customers across the globe.  1.3 ArcelorMittal  Nippon  Steel  India  A joint  venture  between  the ArcelorMittal  Group  and the Nippon  Steel Group,  ArcelorMittal  Nippon  Steel  India (AM/NS  India)  is a leading  integrated  flat carbon  steel  manufacturer  in India - from  iron ore to ready -to-market products – with 
an achievable crude steel capacity of 9 million tonnes per annum (MTPA). Our manufacturing facilities comprise iron 
making, steelmaking, and downstream facilities spread across India. This RFP is being floated by  ArcelorMittal Nippon 
Steel India Private Limited (AM/NS India, alternatively also referred  to as the “Requester” or “AMNS” ).

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\AMNS Weightrack\RFQ\04.07.25- RFP - Logistics Digitization and Automation Mines Phase-2_v2_Legalvetted.pdf

Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  7 | P a g e  2 ABOUT  THE PROJECT  AMNS India, through its Mining Division  is planning to implement Logistics Automation and Digitization Phase -2 solution 
for outbound logistics  for its Mines (“Project”) , to avail  the benefits  that the technology  is popular for. Since  these  are 
mission -critical  applications, the Requester needs to understand what  the risks  would  be involved, how do  the 
prospective  partners  propose to  mitigate  those  risks,  what  would  be the strategies  of implementation  and how these 
systems will be maintained thereafter. The primary objectives of this Project are:  1. Automation and Digitization : To automate and digitize end -to-end logistics operations to improve efficiency  across Organization . 
2. Eliminate Manual Intervention : Our Focus is to  eliminating manual tasks at critical checkpoints such as gate entries, weighbridges, 
Safety Points, and loading points  etc. 
3. Real -Time Vehicle Tracking  (Trace & Track) : Integration of GPS, RFID, and Fas t-tag technologies to enable real -time tracking of 
vehicles  across the mines, during transit and at the delivering plant . 
4. Seamless Interface with Systems : We will ensure smooth communication with i3MS, ULIP, Vahan, m -Parivahan, FOIS, SAP 
S/4HANA, iMHS, and other regulatory and non-regulatory  systems.  5. Central Command and Control Centre : Enhancing the capabilities and efficiency of existing Command and Control Centre for real time monitoring, alerts, analytics, and reporting is crucial.  Detailed analytics, alert and KPI dashboard to be provided.  6. Mobile Application Implementation : A mobile application to be developed for capturing and validating field data and managing 
alerts  for seamless integration . 
7. Improving Compliance : We will enhance transporter and driver compliance using performance metrics and mechanisms for 
blacklisting.  8. Improving Safety:  We will enhance Mines safety using performance metrics and mechanisms to alert on high speeding, rash 
driving, not following the safety compliance etc.  Through this R equest for Proposal (‘R FP’), AMNS intends to evaluate the various potential b idders with respect to its 
requirements and select the one best fitting its requirements. This RFP invites potential b idders, to provide systems 
under consideration, integrating with the existing IT landscape  at AMNS , and ensuring agility & extensibility of the 
resultant system architecture to accommodate  changes  in the future.  Potential  bidders  are henceforth  referred  to as 
“Bidder”  or “Vendor”  in this RFP.

between the Requester and the successful Bidder as determined by the Requester (together, the Parties). The  successful

empanelled/ authorized service providers execute valid contracts with its employees/contractors involved in the 
provision of Services under the Agreement, to ensure compliance by all such individuals with the obligations set out 
under this RFP and the Agreement.

and printing  Auto -documentation  via i3MS and  SAP integration:  • Auto TP  from  i3MS . 
• Auto Invoice  & E-way bill from  SAP. 
• Print  at gate  automatically  with  RFID  t r u c k  ID trigger .  To address the above challenges, it is required to implement state -of-the-art " Outbound Logistics Automation and Digitization " and 
Track and trace solutions  with command -and-control  center . These solutions automate the critical stages of the logistics processes 
using advanced  unified  digital  layers.  The solution  should  eliminate  the manual  processes  and provide  clear  visibility on the entire 
process right from dispatch planning to the delivery at receiving plant and also to integrate with receiving plant existing a pplication 
or to extend the existing Mines system to receiving plant for end-to-end closure and management. The integration directly with 
AMNS’s ERP system (S4 HANA)  and I3MS, eliminates  the need  for manual  data  entry  at various  stages. Various data points across 
the stages  will be automatically  captured  and pushed  to the ERP and I3MS  system,  ensuring  seamless  operations and reducing the 
risk of human error.  The implementation  of the solution  should  significantly  reduce  turnaround  times  (Inside Mines, in route and at receiving Plant) , 
optimize  resource  utilization, and  enhance  process  transparency  through  a robust  digital  layer.  Real -time  tracking of trucks  and 
materials should  provide  end-to-end visibility,  while  automated  systems  will improve  coordination between various 
stakeholders. Furthermore, the solution should capture comprehensive data for audits and reports, enabling AMNS to gain 
actionable insights and maintain a detailed operational record.  Overall,  the adoption  of the solution  should  transform  AMNS’s  mining  operations,  enhancing  efficiency,  reducing costs, and 
providing a modern, automated framework for managing truck and material movements. This system  will address  current  inefficiencies  while  future -proofing  logistics  processes  for sustained  operational excellence.

input. All weighbridge operation activities to be controlled and orchestrated by the system. Various triggers and master sequ ence of 
activities shall be maintained and triggered though the following steps:  ▪ Capturing of Fastag using Fastag readers installed at the weighbridge  ▪ Operationalization of traffic lights for entire set of weighment activities  ▪ Operationalization of Boom Barrier  ▪ Vehicle positioning through sensors and PA System.  ▪ Capturing of truck top view and front view using IP Cameras  ▪ Capturing of stable weight of mineral being carried or tare weight being measured.  ▪ Capturing of gross weight of mineral carrier post loading  ▪ Population of captured data into i3MS system and generation of e -Transit Pass in real time  ▪ RFID tag  authenticated entry into loading point/ area  ▪ Vehicle movement monitoring to pre -assigned loading stack  post tare weighment  ▪ Time stamped vehicle entry and exit data captured at Loading points or stacking points  ▪ Vehicle Entry permission monitoring based on capacity  ▪ Fasttag and GPS based in -mines vehicle tracking  ▪ Time stamped details captured through fasttag scanning . Escalation and Log Management.  ▪ Transporter tags the vehicles in i3MS application.  ▪ After successful gross weighment, e -Transit Pass should be automatically generated in i3MS.  ▪ Data to be synced with SAP for Auto invoice and e -way bill generation  ▪ Auto Printing of TP, E -invoice and E -way Bill through integrated SAP system and LR Copy from this system at exit gate.  ▪ Security to Check and verify all documents through mobile app and allow vehicles to exit the mines.  ▪ Vehicles to exit the mines after receipt of all required documents at exit gate.  ▪ Exit time stamp to be recorded in system. Escalation and Log Management.  ▪ Validation of driver and vehicle by security personnel against data displayed in Mobile /tab (RFID scan). O ther mines receipt 
through QR scan.  ▪ Vehicle Entry permission monitoring based on capacity. Vehicle front and top to be captured through IP Camera.  ▪ No. plate reading by ANPR Camera/Fall back security to enter vehicle no. Vehicle No. to be matched with TP Scan data for 
authorizing plant entry  ▪ Entry Time and security clearance login details to be captured in system  ▪ Vehicle arrives at Gross Weighbridge, other mines → TP scanning. AM/NS Mines digital TP available in system. Gross 
weighment captured.  ▪ Under tolerance digital unloading Slip generated and vehicle move towards unloading point. Beyond tolerance AM/NS to 
approved (Escalation Management) .  ▪ Capturing truck top and front view using IP Camera  ▪ Truck approached to Unloading area → Unloading supervisor notified of vehicle arrival through mobile App, Unloading 
done, entry and exit time recorded with supervisor login details.  ▪ Vehicle moves to Tare WB and TW captured and recorded in system with time and login details.  ▪ Reconciliation of captured data into i3MS system through digital Platform. GRN integration with SAP .  ▪ Vehicles proceed for exit gate. Time and login recorded.  Automation of Documents (TP, E -invoice, E -Way Bill generation)  Vehicle Exit Management  Mineral Receipt at Plant Site  Manned WB at Plant  Loading Point Monitoring

Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  29 | P a g e  5.14 General requirement:  1. Solution should be scalable (without vendor support) to further expansion in location, plant, product, 
distribution /sales  channel,  if any.  AMNS  is operating  at 7.2MTPA  level  currently.  Short /medium  term  plan is to 
ramp up this volume to 14MTPA in next 3 -5 years. There will be volume addition in future as part of long -term 
expansion plan.  2. Planning  horizon  will be 6-12 months  with  weekly  bucket.  Frequency – Monthly /Weekly /any time  as per business 
need.  3. Granularity  should  be at a reasonable  level  where  forecasting  can be done.  4. System  should  be able  to aggregate /disaggregate  forecast  at a required  level  5. Solution  platform /engine  should  be common  for all modules  like logistics  planning,  operations, commercial, 
analytics, finance for Pan India level Mining Operation . 
6. A provision  is to be  provided  for Management  input  in logistics  plan  and facility  to do  variance analysis with 
respect to original logistics plan.  7. Bidder to clearly identify and specify the hardware & software requirements for the project. The Requester 
wishes to keep the project on a cloud platform. Bidder to indicate the expected peak & average  load  on the 
servers.  Bidder  to estimate  and specify  expected  system  running  time  for each module.  8. There  should  be common  master  data  management  for all modules  of the system

Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  30 | P a g e  6 PROJECT  CONSIDERATIONS  6.1 General  Consideration  1. The Bidder  will assign  a 24x7  SPOC  who  has good  understanding  of the system  and mining  industry  supply 
logistics  processes.  2. The Bidder shall deploy 24*7  onsite  team  support  for pre & post implementation.  3. Key stakeholders of the Bidder  including  management  will be available  on a timely  basis  to participate  in 
completion  of the Business Blueprint and Architecture Blueprint and to resolve any issues.  4. During  preparation  of Blueprint  documents,  the Bidder  will be ensuring  the availability of its best  professional 
with relevant expertise  who  will take  total  ownership  of solution  during  the entire  implementation  tenure  5. The Bidder ’s personnel and  consultants  will align timelines and provide extended support whenever  required  to 
achieve the target dates of deliverables.  6. The Requester’s  calendar  will be followed.  7. The Requester  shall have the option to ask for all discussions to happen at  on-site or  off-site. In  case  the Vendor  wants  to have  off-site,  it should  be explicitly  mentioned,  and clearance  should  be taken  from  the Requester 
beforehand.  8. The Bidder  will provide  documented  process  for day-to-day support  post  the hyper -care  support  tenure.  The structure  of the document  will be provided  by The Requester’s  project  manager.  9. Wherever  the Requester’s  related  task or activity  is mentioned,  it will be executed  by The Requester’s  or 
its External  authorized  person/agency.  10. Implementation  of the project,  and subsequent  support  will be in English  language  only.  11. The Bidder  needs to ensure competent, efficient and effective provision of services to minimize time and effort 
and improve on the KPIs identified by the Requester . The Bidder shall ensure that all the personnel, third party 
consultants, system integrator and representative s (‘Personnel’) deployed for the project will be sufficiently 
qualified, skilled and competent with prior experience and knowledge in rendering/ performing similar project. 
The Requester may at its option have a right to request a substitution in the team, if the performance by any 
Personnel is found unsatisfactory. The Bidder shall extend  reasonable support/ assistance in complying with such 
request without any objection. The Requester  and SAP HANA  migration  SI team will be available during the 
System Architecture & Design workshop and Final Solution Validation.  12. Any escalation  from  the Requester  on the project  will be responded to  by Vendor  as per the following  o Sr. Leadership  response  time:  within  one working  days  o Project  Manager:  8 hours on  working  days 
o Team  Lead  & team  members,  response  time:  2 hours  on a  working  day.  4 hours  on non-working days.  6.2 Technical  Considerations  1. Validating  the adopted  architecture  for minimum  costs  and maximum  security  would  be the Vendor 
responsibility.  2. The Vendor’s  technical  team  will have  the technical  skills  necessary  to participate  in the IT Infrastructure  Planning  and carrying  out their  assigned  implementation  tasks.  6.3 Activity  Management  Tools  & Other  Considerations  1. The Bidder  should  use mutually  agreed  upon  tools  for project  / activity  management.  2. The Vendor  will provide  a flow  diagram  showing  the overall  process  flow  of the change  management process, 
problem management process, and service request management process.  3. The Vendor  will provide  an escalation  matrix  with  emails  and contact  details.

Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  31 | P a g e  7 CRITICAL  SUCCESS  FACTORS  1. Visible  participation  of the stakeholders  from  both  sides.  2. Adequate  stakeholder  involvement  in the program  governance  structure  3. Involve  highly  skilled  technical  consultants  4. Automation  of routine  support  tasks  5. Progressive  achievement  of the KPIs. 
6. Clear  definition  of Project  deliverables  and adherence  to Project  Timelines,  Scope,  and Work  Breakdown 
Structures, all agreed upon and frozen in fixed and variable time buckets.  7. Vendor  agrees  to have  proper  senior  management  representation  in a Steering  Committee.  8. Any Scope -Creep  and scope  changes  through  the project duration,  if any,  would  be managed  through the scope 
change request process as detailed in the Agreement, and all such mutual agreements with business should be 
documented and get the sign -off from the Project manager and Steering Committee.  9. Collabora tion and cooperation with other contractors, service providers, third party consultants deployed by 
the Requester at site, to ensure continuous and smooth implementation of the Project.  8 PROJECT  APPROACH,  TIMELINES  & RESOURCES  8.1 Implementation  Approach  • The project shall follow a design, build  and implement, where the Bidder shall help design  the business  solution  basis  discussion  with  various  stakeholders,  build  the corresponding  system  to the best -in-class capability, deploy 
the same onto production systems as provided by AM/NS India . 
• Each  of the modules  as documented  in Section 5 ( Scope  of Work ), shall  be assumed  as a separate  module. The 
Requester has the right to request for specific modules to be provided or prioritized by the Bidder . The final 
solution could be completely provided by one Bidder , or it may be a combination of modules from multiple 
vendors , managed and provided to the Requester by the Bidder . In such cases where modules are provided by 
multiple vendors, the Bidder shall be responsible for coordination and will be the single point of contact for the 
Requester for addressing any co ncerns or resolution of issues.  The Bidder should explicitly mention specific 
modules, of the Bidder’s solution, along with the interdependencies of each module on others.  • The system  can be based  on a private  or public  cloud  environment.  The Bidder  should  clearly  define  the required 
hardware details while mentioning the performance benchmark of the system during the detailing of each 
module.  8.2 Go-Live Support  / Run Phase  • SLA matrix  for issue  resolution.  • 24*7  onsite  support  till system  stabilization  (hyper  care  period)  • Optimize  performance  of the system  based  on usage  during  Hyper  Care  Support.  • Close  all observations  reported  observations  in the Pre-go live and Post  Go-live check  • Resolve  all tickets  raised  by The Requester.  Go-live shall be subject to express approval of the Requester. The purpose of the go -live support phase is to provide 
support for the solution during the period immediately following production cutover, as designated in the Agreement. 
Exceptional items such as additional production support, exceptional busi ness monitoring processes, and extraordinary 
technical support are planned and executed  in this phase.  At the end of the designated  period,  Bidder  is expected  to 
continue  providing  support  in line with the defined SLA in this scope.  In addition,  the disposition  of all issues  encountered  in the transition  to the new  solution  is to be  determined  and
