Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  34 | P a g e  10 OPERATING PRINCIPLES  Project Management : The Vendor will work with the Requester for the project delivery under the current scope of  work.  The Project  will be governed  by a Steering  Committee  as provided under Section 9.1 of this RFP . 
Working  days  in a week: The Vendor shall be required to follow the Requester’s  Calendar  to ensure no interruption in the 
operations of the Requester.  Quality Review:  The Vendor would ensure quality and timely completion of the tasks assigned to its consulting 
resources.  The Requester  shall be entitled to conduct quality  review  during  all phases  of the project.  The Bidder shall 
coordinate and provide necessary access to  its systems and records  quality review  by the Requester .  Project Scope : In the interest of timely implementation of the Project within planned costs, it is important to manage 
and contain the project scope within desired limits and areas only. The high -level requirements  as provided in Section 5 
(Scope of Work)  of this RFP are indicative and the final  scope  and deliverable  of this  project  will be the mutually  agreed  upon  during  the detailed  Blue  Print  Phase  and documented  as a part of signed  off Blueprint,  and the Vendor  shall  be fully  governed  by this Blue  Print  (which shall form an integral part of the Agreement)  as well as the terms  of this RFP.  The 
reason for dropping or adding a scope item has to be routed through PMO and Steering committee.  Resource  Deployment : Resource  deployment  has to be done  keeping  in mind  the timelines  to be  achieved  under 
different phases of the project and other integration points. In the interest of quality, continuity of resources on the 
project is of utmost importance. In case any of the resources have to be offloaded, a minimum of 1 -month prior 
permission from the Requester’s Project Manager is required in writ ing and if this is a regular occurrence, an adequate 
amount of LD will be imposed on Vendor.  The Project Managers of the Requester and Vendor will come together to review the resource requirement periodically  and take  decisions  of reducing/increasing  the resources deployed.  This will help  to further  optimize the  resource  deployment.  A calendar  for such  review  will be worked  out and communicated  to all concerned  to facilitate participation 
on time.  System  Administration : The Vendor  will identify  and provide full -time  system  administration  and system  support to  the 
project team  after  go-live. Necessary  hands -on training for the system  administration  team will  be planned in the project. 
Vendor  will provide expert advice on technology requirements on a need -basis during the course of the project.  Documentation  Scope:  Provide  end-to-end documentation  of the complete  project  deliverable,  and the SOPs  for the 
day-to-day maintenance activities.  Post -Go-Live Support:  The Vendor  will provide  24*7  on-site Post -Go-Live Support  for the duration  indicated  in Project 
Timelines.  Project Communication: While the Requester shall drive the communication, the Vendor will assist in its formulation  and on a need  basis,  help  conduct  senior  management  briefings,  provide  content  for newsletters,  etc. 
Change  Management  and Process  Improvement : The Vendor  team  will help  identify  “Process”  areas  needing  the 
attention of change management and through the modes of communication and training, address the Package enabled 
change management to process owners and end -users.  Continuity of services : The Vendor has to ensure compliance with all Applicable Laws, including any restrictions provided 
under the Applicable Laws  and as amended from time to time to ensure continuity of the Services.

shall be borne by the Vendor. Even in the instance the Vendor’s personnel are present on the premises  of the Requester  for the purposes  of the provision  of the services  contemplated  under  this Agreement, the Vendor shall remain 
responsible for the actions of  its personnel, and for any accidents that these individuals may suffer.  12.4 Confidentiality  Neither Party will share / disclose the  confidential information of the other Party  received pursuant to this RFP or the 
Agreement . The Parties shall be responsible for ensuring  confidentiality  and to limit the disclosure of confidential 
information on a strict need to know basis. Any disclosure to any third party shall be subject to prior consent of the party 
owning such confidential information. The obligations of con fidentiality shall also survive the termination  of the 
Agreement.  The Requester  shall,  in any event,  be permitted to disclose such confidential information to its affiliates and 
prospective investors.  12.5 Governing  Law and Jurisdiction  The Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India and subject to the dispute resolution  mechanism below, the courts 
in Mumbai  shall have the  exclusive  jurisdiction  to settle  any dispute  arising  out of or in connection  with  it. All disputes 
between the Parties arising out of this Contract shall be settled in accordance with the arbitration rules of the Mumbai 
Centre for International Arbitration (“MCIA Rules”) for the time being in force, which rules are deemed to be 
incorporated  by reference in this clau se. 
The arbitration shall be conducted in English, and the place and seat of the arbitration shall be Mumbai, Maharashtra, 
India. The arbitration shall be conducted by an arbitration tribunal consisting of three (3) arbitrators. Each Party shall 
appoint one (1 ) arbitrator who shall in turn, appoint a third arbitrator ("Tribunal").  Notwithstanding the reference of any dispute to be resolved through arbitration pursuant to this Clause, the Parties 
shall continue to perform their respective obligations under the Contract, unless they otherwise agree.  12.6 Change  in law 
In case  of any change in  Applicable law,  the parties shall mutually discuss in good faith to reach an agreement on an 
economical and commercially prudent method for complying with such change. In case of any such change in law having 
a material adverse impact on the performance under the Agreement and the Parties not being able to reach  an 
agreement  within  a specified  time  period  as may be mutually agreed , then the Requester shall have a right to terminate 
the Agreement by written notice to the Vendor.  12.7 Term,  Termination,  and Suspension  The vendor  shall  provide  the services  as described  in this RFP and as agreed under the Agreement to the Requester  starting  from  the effective date as agreed in the Agreement and shall continue to do so in accordance with the terms of 
the Agreement.  12.7.1  Termination  for Convenience  The Requester  shall have a right under the Agreement to  terminate  the Agreement  for convenience  upon  providing  prior 
notice to the Vendor .

relief as it may deem necessary as per the terms of the Agreement . 
12.8 Force  Majeure  (a) The term “ Force Majeure ” shall mean an event or circumstance which is beyond the reasonable control of the 
affected Party (“ Affected Party ”), and which (a) is on the critical path of timely completion of the project, in whole 
or in part or of the identified milestones , (b) the Affected Party could not reasonably have provided against (not 
including via financial means) or foreseen before entering into the Agreement  despite exercising reasonable due 
diligence, (c) having arisen, such Affected Party could not  reasonably have avoided or overcome, (d) is not 
attributable to the fault or negligence of the Affected Party, its personnel , third -party service providers or sub contractors .  (b) Either Party shall not be liable for any failure to perform or delay in the performance of its obligations under this 
Agreement , and will not be in breach of this Agreement , if and to the extent that its performance is prevented, 
impeded or delayed by a Force Majeure event. However the Affected Party shall be responsible to undertake 
commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of the Force Majeure event.  (c) Upon the occurrence of a Force Majeure event, the Affected Party shall notify the other Party of such occurrence, 
describing the Force Majeure event and the obligations the performance of which have been prevented, hindered 
or delayed thereby.  (d) If a Force Majeure event occurs and its effect continues for a continuous period of 90 days , the Buyer may give to 
the Contractor, a notice of termination, which shall take effect 1 5 days after the giving of the notice , without  any 
cost or liability to the Buyer on account of such termination.  12.9 Indemnity  General Indemnity  The Bidder  shall fully indemnify, hold harmless and defend the Requester , its affiliates, personnel and the directors,

Project  Name:  Logistics Automation and Digitization – Phase -2  Document  Name:  RFP – Logistics Automation & Digitization  for AM/NS  India,  Mining  44 | P a g e  ANNEXURE  B: ADDITIONAL  SOX REQUIREMENTS  1. Establish  safeguards  to prevent  data  tampering  (Section  302.2)  Track  user  login  access  to all computers  that contain  sensitive  data  and detect  break -in attempts  to computers, 
databases, fixed and removable storage, and websites.  2. Establish  safeguards  to establish  timelines.  (Section  302.3)  Timestamp  all data  as it is received  in real-time.  This data  should  be stored  at a remote  location  as soon  as it is received, 
thereby preventing data alteration or loss. In addition, log information should be moved to a secure location and an 
encrypted MD5 checksum created, thereby preventing any tampering.  3. Establish  verifiable  controls  to track  data  access.  (Section  302.4.B)  Receive  data  messages from  virtually  an unlimited  number  of sources.  Collection  of data  should  be supported from  file 
queues,  FTP transfers,  and databases,  independent  of the actual  framework  used,  such  as COBIT  and ISO/IEC 27000.  4. Ensure  that safeguards  are operational.  (Section  302.4.C)  Issue  daily  reports to  e-mail  addresses  and distribute  reports  via RSS,  making  it easy  to verify  that the system  is up and 
running from any location.  5. Periodically  report  the effectiveness  of safeguards.  (Section  302.4.D)  Generate  multiple  types  of reports,  including  a report  on all messages,  critical  messages, alerts  and uses a ticketing 
system that archives what security problems and activities have occurred.  6. Detect  Security  Breaches.  (Section  302.5.A/B)  Perform  semantic  analysis  of messages  in real-time  and use correlation  threads,  counters,  alerts,  and triggers  that refine 
and reduce incoming messages into high -level alerts. These alerts then generate tickets that list the  security breach, 
send out an email, or update an incident management system.  7. Disclose  security  safeguards  to SOX auditors.  (Section  404.A.1.1)  Provide access to auditors using role -based permissions. Auditors may be permitted complete access to specific reports  and facilities  without  the ability  to actually  make  changes  to these  components  or reconfigure  the system.  8. Disclose  security  breaches  to SOX auditors.  (Section  404.A.2)  Detect  and log security  breaches,  notifying  security  personnel  in real-time,  and permit  the resolution  to security incidents 
to be entered and stored. All input messages are to be continuously correlated to create tickets that record security 
breaches and other events.  9. Disclose  failures  of security  safeguards  to SOX auditors.  (Section  404.B)  Periodically  test network  and file integrity  and verify  that messages  are logged.  Ideally  the system  interfaces  with 
common security test software and port scanners to verify that the system is successfully monitoring IT security.

Currently, AMNS is handling its entire mining operations manually right from the dispatch planning till the vehicles carrying the minerals reaches the Dabuna Plant. The entire mining operations involves the coordination and exchange of information and data at real-time between various government and local stakeholders at every stage of the process. The heavy reliance on manual processes and the lack of real time communication between the fragmented work flows and data silos is creating an inefficiency in the entire process.
Key Challenges in As-is process:
Heavy reliance on manual processes at all the levels of the process.
Lack of visibility on the Turn-around time at  all the stages.
Unnecessary delays across the stages because of manual processes
Revenue losses owing to theft and pilferages.
Inefficient process flows and inaccurate data flow through the stages.
Lower productivity of resources because of manual intervention.
Lack of co-ordination and communication with stakeholders.
Proposed Solution:
To address above challenges, we propose implementing our state-of-the-art "Connected Plant Logistics" (CPL) and Track and trace solutions. These solutions automate the critical stages of the logistics processes using advanced unified digital layers. The solution will eliminate the manual processes and provide clear visibility on the entire process right from dispatch planning to the Epod. The integration directly with AMNS’s ERP system and I3MS, eliminates the need for manual data entry at various stages. Various data points across the stages will be automatically captured and pushed to the ERP and I3MS system, ensuring seamless operations and reducing the risk of human error.
The implementation of the solution will significantly reduce turnaround times, optimize resource utilization, and enhance process transparency through a robust digital layer. Real-time tracking of trucks and materials will provide end-to-end visibility, while automated systems will improve coordination between various stakeholders. Furthermore, the solution will capture comprehensive data for audits and reports, enabling AMNS to gain actionable insights and maintain a detailed operational record.
Overall, the adoption of the solution will transform AMNS’s mining operations, enhancing efficiency, reducing costs, and providing a modern, automated framework for managing truck and material movements. This system will address current inefficiencies while future-proofing logistics processes for sustained operational excellence.

We have a three-layered approach to Governance
Steering committee and value board: This layer will have the executive sponsor for the project with one-to-one mapping with the AMNS’s Sponsors level management. The team will meet monthly/quarterly to validate and monitor the project progress and take strategic decisions. This layer is the highest level of escalation,
Engagement & project management: This layer provides the project level monitoring and management. They will deal with the day-to-day operation issues and escalations. This layer will be responsible for the project success and excellence.
Project operations & transformation: This layer involves all the members of execution team including Technical SPOCs, functional SPOCs and SMEs. These will interact with the client project team on daily basis and work for the success of the project.

The product roadmap has been built keeping current and future needs of the industry in scope. The roadmap establishes our commitment to the innovations and capabilities which we co-develop with client to ensure these developments are relevant to the needs of the clients.
The intent behind this vision is simple. We understand that “connectivity” is the way for future and hence the core of any solution must embrace the connected part and should support the mechanisms which promotes connectivity.
We understand that while vision is clear, we also need to keep the structural elements flexible and modular. Hence, the design of our architecture and roadmap has been envisioned to be ‘modular and agile’.

AM/NS PUNE .  •  1 | P a g e  Request for Proposal (RFP): Steel Yard Management – Real -Time Yard Management 
Solution for Steel Coils  1. Introduction and Purpose  AM/NS India  is seeking proposals from qualified vendors for the design, development, and 
implementation of a "Steel Yard Management " – a real -time yard management solution for steel 
Coils. This solution aims to revolutionize our steel Coil inventory and logistics operations by 
leveraging cutting -edge technology to enhance visibility, efficiency, and safety within our storage 
yards.  The primary objective of this RFP is to select a partner capable of delivering a comprehensive, 
scalable, and robust system that meets the detailed requirements outlined herein. We are looking 
for an innovative solution that minimizes manual intervention, reduces operational costs, and 
provides actionable insights for optimized yard management.  2. Project Background  Currently, our steel Coil yard management relies on a combination of manual processes and 
systems, leading to challenges such as:  • Limited real -time visibility into Coil locations and inventory.  • Increased time and effort for Coil retrieval and placement.  • Higher risk of misplacement or damages  of steel Coils. 
• Difficulties in tracking aging inventory and ensuring compliance.  The Steel Yard Management  solution will address these pain points by introducing a digital, real time, and intelligent system to improve operational efficiency and decision -making.  3. Scope of Work  The selected vendor will be responsible for the end -to-end delivery of the Steel Yard Management  solution, including but not limited to:  3.1. Core System Features  • Real -time Positioning:  Implementation of a system for real-time positioning of each steel 
Coil within the yard using suitable tagging/sensor technologies (e.g., RFID, GPS, UWB).  • Steel Coil Tracking:  o Assignment of a unique ID  to each steel Coil. 
o Comprehensive tracking of movement history, condition, batch information, and 
grade . 
• Inventory Dashboard:  A centralized, live dashboard providing real-time inventory updates  with advanced filtering capabilities (e.g., by grade, size, production line, aging, and status).

AM/NS PUNE .  •  2 | P a g e  3.2. Integration Capabilities  • Seamless integration with existing enterprise systems, including:  o ERP systems  (e.g., SAP  – S4 HANA ). 
o Logistics modules . 
3.3. Alerts & Notifications  • Configuration and delivery of real -time alerts and notifications for critical events, including:  o Misplaced Coil alerts . 
o Aging inventory notifications . 
o Unauthorized movement detection . 
4. Technical Requirements  Vendors should propose a robust and scalable technology stack that aligns with modern industry 
best practices. vendors are encouraged to propose solution that offer s superior value or capabilities.  5. Deliverables  The selected vendor will be responsible for delivering the following:  • Detailed project plan and timelines.  • System design documentation (architecture, database schema, API specifications).  • Developed and fully tested Steel Yard Management  Solution . 
• Integrated and supply of all hardware components (tagging, scanners, etc.)  & System 
connectivity (i.e. Wi -Fi / Networking etc.)  • Supply of any manpower for operation of this solution.  • Integration with specified ERP and Logistics systems.  • Comprehensive user manuals and technical documentation.  • Training for our staff on system operation and administration.  • Deployment and go -live support.  • Post -implementation support and maintenance plan.  • Inventory reconciliation  to be done for Physical & System Stocks  6. Benefits  The successful implementation of the Steel Yard Management  solution is expected to yield 
significant benefits, including:

AM/NS PUNE .  •  3 | P a g e  • Increased Yard Visibility:  Real -time insights into the location and status of every steel Coil. 
• Reduction in Coil Retrieval Time:  Optimized placement and accurate location data will 
significantly speed up retrieval processes.  • Minimized Loss/Misplacement:  Enhanced tracking and alert systems will drastically reduce 
lost or misplaced inventory.  • Better Compliance & Safety:  Improved adherence to storage regulations and safer yard 
operations.  • Scalability:  The solution should be scalable to accommodate growth and adaptable for other 
yards or materials beyond steel Coils. 
7. Proposal Submission Requirements  Interested vendors are requested to submit a comprehensive proposal that includes, but is not 
limited to, the following sections:  • Executive Summary:  A high -level overview of your proposed solution and how it meets our 
needs.  • Company Profile:  Information about your company, relevant experience, and qualifications.  • Proposed Solution:  A detailed description of your proposed Steel Yard Management  solution, including:  o Approach to each feature outlined in Section 3.  o Proposed hardware and software architecture.  o Specific technologies to be used.  o User experience interfaces.  o Approach to system integration.  • Project Plan:  Detailed project timelines, milestones, and resource allocation.  • Team Composition:  Resumes of key personnel who will be assigned to this project.  • References:  At least three client references for similar projects.  • Cost Proposal:  A detailed breakdown of all costs, including development, hardware, 
software licenses, implementation, training, and ongoing support.  All Hardware & Software 
will have to be supported with Manufacturer’s Guarantee / warranty .  • Support & Maintenance:  Outline of your proposed post -implementation support and 
maintenance services  with specific  timeline . 
• Risk Management:  Identification of potential risks and proposed mitigation strategies.  • Safety Protocol :  All safety protocols & requirements to be followed during execution of 
project.

• Reduction in Coil Retrieval Time:  Optimized placement and accurate location data will 
significantly speed up retrieval processes.  • Minimized Loss/Misplacement:  Enhanced tracking and alert systems will drastically reduce 
lost or misplaced inventory.  • Better Compliance & Safety:  Improved adherence to storage regulations and safer yard 
operations.  • Scalability:  The solution should be scalable to accommodate growth and adaptable for other 
yards or materials beyond steel Coils. 
7. Proposal Submission Requirements  Interested vendors are requested to submit a comprehensive proposal that includes, but is not 
limited to, the following sections:  • Executive Summary:  A high -level overview of your proposed solution and how it meets our 
needs.  • Company Profile:  Information about your company, relevant experience, and qualifications.  • Proposed Solution:  A detailed description of your proposed Steel Yard Management  solution, including:  o Approach to each feature outlined in Section 3.  o Proposed hardware and software architecture.  o Specific technologies to be used.  o User experience interfaces.  o Approach to system integration.  • Project Plan:  Detailed project timelines, milestones, and resource allocation.  • Team Composition:  Resumes of key personnel who will be assigned to this project.  • References:  At least three client references for similar projects.  • Cost Proposal:  A detailed breakdown of all costs, including development, hardware,

project.

AM/NS PUNE .  •  4 | P a g e  8. Evaluation Criteria  Proposals will be evaluated based on the following criteria:  • Vendor Experience and Qualifications:  Demonstrated expertise in IoT, real -time systems, 
and similar industrial solutions.  • Technical Solution:  Innovativeness, robustness, scalability, and adherence to technical 
requirements.  • Project Methodology and Plan:  Clarity, feasibility, and comprehensiveness of the proposed 
project plan.  • Cost -Effectiveness:  Overall value for money.  • Support and Maintenance Plan:  Quality and responsiveness of proposed post implementation support.  • References:  Client testimonials and project success stories.  9. Value -added option s 
You may also offer :  • Any improvised module s to suggest optimal storage locations  for incoming Coils based on 
parameters such as size, dispatch urgency, and material type.  • Development  of Visual layout of the yard , accurately reflecting the current state and 
arrangement of steel Coils, with photos  etc.  We look forward to receiving your innovative proposals for the Steel Yard Management  project.

of a Party's negligence or the failure of such Party to 
perform any of its obligations under this Agreement, and

describing such event, the effec t thereof and the actions

under this Agreement after the termination of the Force 
Majeure and the actions being  taken.  ➢ Instances of Force Majeure. Subject to the 
provisions, Events of Force Majeure shall include, but not

breakdowns, embargoes or delays caused or resulting 
from willful act or omission by or on the part of such party

Dear AMNSTeam,
We are pleased to submit this response to your request for quotation to provide AMNS with services for Digital yard Management System. Our team has invested significant effort, deliberation, and diligence into preparing this proposal and we are confident that it conveys our sincere commitment to partner with you, as well as our understanding of how Axestrack can deliver the most cost-effective business value to the organization.
We always seek to do the right thing for our clients. Throughout the subsequent materials, we are confident that we will demonstrate our pursuit of excellence in service at an optimized end-to-end economic value. With the goal of driving success to AMNS & add value to its technology ecosystem, we put our strengths to build what we consider a best-of-breed solution for AMNS.

AMNS, as a leading steel manufacturer, operates extensive yards to store and handle high-value steel coils that are critical to its production and supply chain operations. Efficient yard management is essential to ensure timely dispatches, minimize operational costs, and maintain product quality. However, the current processes face several operational challenges that hinder productivity and create avoidable delays.

Lack of Real-Time Tracking: Inability to monitor the exact location and movement of coils within the yard, leading to inefficiencies.
Misplaced Coils: Difficulty in identifying misplaced coils, resulting in operational delays.
Loading Delays: Increased truck and wagon turnaround times due to untraceable coils during dispatch operations.
Coil Ageing: Ineffective inventory rotation, leading to ageing coils and potential quality concerns.
To address these challenges, Axestrack proposes an advanced Digital Yard Management Solution that leverages Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) technology and AI-powered analytics to transform AMNS’s yard operations into a streamlined, data-driven system.

Graphical representation:
BLE-Based Real-Time Tracking: Deployment of BLE tags on coils combined with strategically placed BLE gateways to provide precise, real-time visibility of all coils in the yard.
AI-Driven Optimization: Intelligent algorithms to suggest optimal stacking, retrieval, and dispatch plans ensuring FIFO/LIFO compliance and minimal coil movement.
Automated Misplacement Alerts: Proactive notifications to operational teams in case of misplaced or wrongly stacked coils.
Accelerated Loading Operations: Guided retrieval processes for faster and error-free truck/wagon loading, reducing turnaround time.
Centralized Yard Dashboard: Live yard maps, KPI dashboards, and actionable insights for improved operational visibility and control.
Seamless ERP Integration: Ensures smooth data exchange between the Yard Management System and AMNS’s existing ERP platforms.

Graphical representation:
This solution uses RFID technology to accurately identify and update the location of steel coils in the yard. The process is as follows:
Tagging the RFID with the Coil: As soon as the Quality Check (QC) of a coil is completed, the operator at the packing yard maps an RFID tag with the Unique ID of the coil using a handheld RFID and barcode reader.
C-hook Engages Coil: When the C-hook engages a coil to shift it to the yard, the RFID reader strategically mounted on the hook detects the tag and retrieves the associated Unique ID of the coil.
Capture X-Y Coordinates: As the crane moves, RFID tags and readers placed on the crane girder and its rails continuously track the X-Y movements of the crane on the yard grid. This data is sent to the central dashboard in real-time.
Movement Tracking: When the coil is placed in a new location, the same process captures the updated X-Y coordinates and updates the coil’s location on the central dashboard.

40% reduction in retrieval and loading times.
Improved yard space utilization and organized stacking.
Elimination of manual searching, leading to higher labor productivity.
Minimized inventory ageing and quality risks through automated coil rotation management.
Enhanced decision-making with real-time operational insights and analytics.

Axestrack will deploy a robust real-time positioning system using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE)/RFID gateways and tags strategically placed across the yard. Each steel coil will be tagged with a BLE beacon/RFID, enabling accurate detection of its current position within the yard grid. The system ensures:
Live visibility of all coils on a digital yard map.
High precision location updates with minimal infrastructure costs compared to GPS or UWB.
Scalable architecture to cover both open and covered yard areas effectively.

Each steel coil will be assigned a unique digital ID, linked to its physical BLE tag/RFID.
The system will record and maintain a complete movement history of the coil within the yard, ensuring traceability from production to dispatch.
Metadata such as batch number, grade, size, and condition will be integrated into the tracking system to provide a 360-degree view of each asset.

A centralized, web-based dashboard will provide real-time inventory insights, enabling operations teams to monitor and manage the yard efficiently.
Advanced filtering and search capabilities will allow users to query inventory based on grade, size, production line, coil ageing, and movement status.
KPI widgets (e.g., yard occupancy, dwell time, ageing coils) will enable data-driven decision-making and proactive yard management.

The system will integrate with SAP S/4HANA and other ERP platforms through REST APIs or middleware. This will enable:
Automated data exchange (e.g., coil creation, dispatch orders).
Synchronized inventory records between the yard system and enterprise systems.

Integration with logistics modules will provide visibility into inbound/outbound vehicle movements and enable real-time planning of coil loading/unloading based on dispatch schedules.
This open API architecture ensures flexibility for future integrations with warehouse, transport, and production systems.

Axestrack’s solution will incorporate a robust alerts and notifications engine, configurable for key operational events:
Misplaced Coil Alerts: Immediate notifications when a coil is stacked or moved to an unauthorized location, preventing operational delays and rework.
Aging Inventory Notifications: Automated alerts for coils exceeding predefined dwell time thresholds, ensuring FIFO/LIFO compliance and preventing quality degradation.
Unauthorized Movement Detection: Real-time alerts for any coil movement that is not part of an authorized workflow, enhancing yard security.
Notifications can be delivered via:
Mobile App push notifications for on-ground supervisors.
Email/SMS alerts for management teams.
Dashboard pop-ups for control room operators.

Issues and bugs related to third-party products, services, or plugins are out of scope. Axestrack will only assist in identifying such issues.
Office facility & working space will be provided by AMNS for Axestrack’s onsite team.
Network, security, IT, and other infrastructure will be managed by AMNS. Axestrack will provide only recommendations related to these areas and will be out of scope.

Our solution is built on a robust multi-tenant SaaS architecture, leveraging the scalability and security of Amazon Web Services (AWS) to deliver a high-performing and reliable platform. This architecture allows us to serve multiple customers (tenants) from a shared infrastructure while ensuring data isolation, configurability, and consistent performance.

A single instance of the application serves multiple tenants.
Each tenant’s data is logically isolated using tenant identifiers at the database and application layers.
Shared resources such as compute, storage, and networking are optimized to support multiple users without impacting performance.

Deployed on AWS using services such as:
Amazon EC2/ECS/EKS for compute resources.
Amazon RDS/Aurora for database management with tenant-aware schemas.
Amazon S3 for object storage.
AWS CloudFront for global content delivery and faster load times.
AWS Cognito for secure user authentication and authorization.
AWS VPC and Security Groups for network and data security.

Auto-scaling groups adjust resources dynamically based on workload.
Multi-AZ deployments and load balancing ensure high availability and fault tolerance.
Customization for Tenants
Supports tenant-specific configurations like branding, workflows, and access controls while maintaining a unified codebase.

Cost Efficiency: Users benefit from a shared infrastructure model, reducing the cost of ownership compared to a single-tenant system.
Seamless Updates: All tenants receive automatic updates and enhancements without downtime or disruption.
Scalability: The architecture can scale horizontally to support growing user bases without performance degradation.
Data Security & Privacy: Each tenant’s data is logically isolated, ensuring privacy and compliance with data protection standards.
High Availability: AWS infrastructure ensures 99.99% uptime with disaster recovery capabilities, giving users uninterrupted access to the platform.
Performance Optimization: With AWS’s global presence and CDN (CloudFront), users enjoy fast response times regardless of location.
Customizability: Tenants can customize their experience within the shared application framework, from UI branding to role-based access controls.
Compliance Ready: Leveraging AWS’s compliance certifications (ISO, SOC, GDPR, etc.), our platform aligns with global regulatory requirem

The Apcotex’s outbound dispatch process is predominantly manual and lacks system integration, resulting in operational delays and limited transparency. After the dispatch plan is finalized, transporters receive intimation through email and arrange vehicles accordingly. On arrival at the plant, security personnel carry out document verification manually before granting entry. Vehicles then move to the weighbridge and loading areas where weighment, loading instructions, and related data are recorded on paper, with no connection to the SAP system. Invoicing is also performed manually, adding to processing time and increasing the risk of errors or discrepancies.
Once vehicles enter the plant, there is no digital visibility of their location or status at various stages such as waiting, loading, or post-loading checks. This makes it difficult to identify bottlenecks, track turnaround time (TAT), or monitor loading productivity. After dispatch, the absence of a unified tracking mechanism and the fragmented transporter network further result in no real-time visibility of vehicles while in transit. Similarly, for inbound movements, there is no real-time visibility of trucks carrying critical raw materials, leading to poor planning, delays in material availability, and hampered operational efficiency. Additionally, the manual checks at the gate are time-consuming, further slowing down the overall supply chain.
Key Challenges
Entirely manual gate security checks, weighbridge operations, and loading processes with no SAP integration
No digital visibility of vehicle movement or stage-wise status inside the plant, leading to hidden bottlenecks
Inability to monitor TAT, loading efficiency, or productivity in real time
Manual invoicing that prolongs processing and increases the risk of errors
Lack of real-time vehicle tracking on outbound routes due to a fragmented transporter network
No real-time visibility of inbound truck movement for critical raw materials, causing poor planning and operational inefficiencies
Lengthy and manual gate verification process that delays both inbound and outbound movements

The proposed “Unified Digital Logistics Solution” replaces manual tasks with automated, real-time workflows for both outbound, inbound and in plant movements. Dispatch planning is executed on a centralized platform linked to SAP, where transporters receive dispatch schedules and upload vehicle and driver details before arrival. Pre-arrival validation checks the documents—such as driver license, vehicle fitness certificate, and insurance—against predefined rules, enabling automatic approval or flagging exceptions. Once validated, the system generates a digital gate pass and sends an entry slot confirmation to both transporter and plant security.
At the plant gate, vehicles are authenticated through RFID and Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR), drastically reducing entry time and eliminating paper-based verification. Inside the plant, IoT sensors installed strategically at various stages provide live visibility of vehicle movement across all stages—waiting, loading, and weighbridge operations. Weighbridge data flows directly to SAP, triggering automated loading confirmation and digital invoicing.
For inbound logistics, the same platform tracks raw-material trucks from source to plant and share accurate ETAs. Pre-arrival validation ensures all driver and vehicle documents are approved before gate entry, preventing delays and supporting better production planning. Real-time dashboards give operations and management teams complete oversight of TAT, bottlenecks, fleet utilization, and transporter performance, driving proactive decisions and continuous efficiency gains.
Key Benefits
Pre-Arrival Validation: Automated verification of driver and vehicle documents ensures compliance and speeds up gate entry.
End-to-End Visibility: Live tracking of inbound and outbound vehicles, both in transit and inside the plant.
SAP & Weighbridge Integration: Automated weighbridge data capture and digital invoicing reduce manual errors.
Faster Gate Operations: Digital gate passes and RFID/ANPR authentication minimize waiting time.
Optimized TAT & Planning: Accurate ETAs and real-time stage monitoring improve scheduling, inventory control, and overall productivity.

Currently, Asahi Glass India Ltd. relies heavily on manual processes for tracking pallets within and outside its plant and warehouse. These processes depend significantly on human intervention at key system touchpoints, such as gate entries, pallet movement checks, and compliance validations. This results in inefficiencies and inconsistencies across operations.
The diagram below illustrates the current As-is flow at Asahi India Glass.
The absence of standardized procedures across multiple parallel processes leads to high turnaround times (TAT), especially at the plant gate. Pallet tracking is uneven and prone to errors, contributing to an annual loss of approximately 10% of pallets. Compliance checks are insufficient across various stages, and the lack of a unified tracking system makes real-time monitoring impossible.
Additionally, excessive reliance on paperwork and administrative staff input slows down operations, creates bottlenecks, and compromises the accuracy of records. The absence of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) further exacerbates the challenges, as productivity and process automation goals remain unmeasured and unoptimized.

With Axestrack's "Connected Plant Logistics" (CPL) deployed, the process will transform into a streamlined and technology-driven system. RFID readers, both static and handheld, will be installed at critical checkpoints such as gates, the pallet area, and warehouses, enabling automated and accurate pallet tracking. Each pallet will be equipped with two anti-metal RFID tags, ensuring comprehensive tracking and eliminating discrepancies.
The diagram below illustrates the To-be flow progression through various checkpoints for Asahi India Glass
Manual checks will be replaced with automated data capture and real-time updates integrated with Asahi's ERP system, providing centralized control and real-time visibility through a command center. Digitalized documentation will replace paper-based processes, significantly reducing administrative overhead. TAT will be tracked and optimized with a breakdown by process steps, allowing for bottleneck identification and resolution.
Compliance checks will become more robust and consistent across all stages, ensuring process standardization and adherence to protocols. The benefits of this transformation are significant: operational efficiency will increase with reduced TAT and bottlenecks, pallet losses will decline due to accurate tracking, and real-time visibility will enhance decision-making.
Standardized processes will ensure consistency, while automation will free up administrative staff for higher-value tasks. The deployment of CPL will provide Asahi Glass with a scalable and future-ready logistics system, delivering measurable improvements in productivity, cost savings, and overall process reliability.

CPL will address the core requirements of Asahi India Glass by providing the following functionalities:
Phase 1: Initial Scope (Pallet Visibility at Multiple Locations)
RFID Infrastructure Deployment
Locations: Loading docks, storage areas, entry/exit points of each facility
Handheld RFID readers to the floor team for manual scanning of pallets when needed.
Pallet Visibility Software – Connected Plant Logistics
Pallet RFID Tagging, dashboard, and integration with existing systems (ERP/WMS).
Dashboard & Reports
Data Capture and Management
Record and log every pallet movement, including arrival, storage, and dispatch, using RFID data capture.
Mapping of all key locations within the plant and warehouses where pallets are stored.
Installation and commissioning of all required hardware and software components
Executing CPL Development and Deployment
Executing System Integration Testing (SIT) and facilitate User Acceptance Testing (UAT)
Post Go-live Hypercare Support (1 Months)
Key User Training through Train the Trainer approach
Comprehensive documentation and support for audits, reports, and future scalability.
Phase 2: Initial Scope Future Scope (Digitalization and Control Tower for Pallet Management)
Handheld RFID Deployment
Deployment of Handheld RFID Readers at Customer Locations
Pallet Status Reporting for pallet validation
Automated Pallet Return Management Dashboard
Return Timeline Management through set predefined return timelines for pallets (e.g., 30 days from dispatch).
Automated Alerts and Notifications
Managed Control Tower:
Centralized monitoring and management of pallet status across the entire supply chain.
Real Time Intervention for Pallet Status
Reporting & Analytics
Pallet Status Reporting for pallet stock levels, return rates, overdue pallets, and other relevant KPIs.
Advanced Analytics to analyze pallet utilization, stock turnover rates, and identifying potential inefficiencies in the system.
Key User Training through ‘Train the Trainer’ Approach
Note: Phase 2 implementation is not included in the proposal document and will be estimated separately upon mutual agreement.

Seamless Integration with ERP through API
Establish a real-time interface for bidirectional data exchange
Pull required fields from ERP to the CPL application for validation

ROI Impact Summary

YMS
Business Case – Yard Management System (YMS)
 Ashok Leyland – Alwar RSO
Objective
 Digitise yard operations at Alwar RSO (Yard 1, Yard 2 & Bus Yard) to reduce manual effort, improve retrieval efficiency, and bring full VIN-level traceability across ~4,200 vehicles.
Current Challenges
 • Only line-level parking recorded; no exact slot visibility
 • Driver memory–based retrieval across ~4,000 truck/bus slots
 • Frequent re-parking, rework movement, & yard shifting not tracked
 • Difficult VIN retrieval during FIFO dispatch
 • No real-time yard capacity view (free/occupied slots)
 • Buses move Production → RSO Yard → Bodywork Vendor → Bus Yard, with no digital trace
 • High time-to-retrieve, congestion, and unproductive labour effort
Proposed Solution – Axestrack Yard Management System
 • VIN-based digital intake & slot-level tracking (GPS/lat-long)
 • Real-time yard map for Yard 1, Yard 2, Bus Yard
 • Navigation-based retrieval for drivers
 • FIFO-based dispatch automation
 • Movement history: parking → re-parking → rework → dispatch
 • Driver/Jockey productivity analytics
 • Yard utilisation dashboards
Commercials
 • Yard 1: INR 1,35,000 per month
 • Yard 2: INR 1,35,000 per month
 Total – INR 2,70,000 per month (Bus Yard optional addition)
ROI Snapshot (Conservative Estimate)
1. Retrieval Time Savings
 • Current: 30–45 minutes per vehicle
 • Post-YMS: 5–10 minutes
 → 45–60 percent reduction
 Assuming 50–80 retrievals per day:
 20–30 labour hours saved daily
 = ₹3–4.5 lakh per month saved (driver + supervisor time)
2. Yard Utilisation Improvement
 • 15–20 percent better slot utilisation
 → Avoids overflow, improves dispatch readiness
 Estimated impact: ₹1.5–2 lakh per month (reduced re-parking, optimised flow)
3. Reduced Misplacements / Avoidable Movements
 • Eliminates duplicate movements & wrong-parked vehicles
 → ₹0.5–1 lakh per month indirect savings
4. Faster Dispatch → Faster Billing
 • 15–25 percent improvement in dispatch throughput
 → Even a 1-day reduction in billing cycle for 50–100 vehicles
 = ₹2–3 lakh per month cash-flow advantage
Total Monthly ROI Potential
₹8.5 lakh – ₹10 lakh per month
 vs.
 Subscription Cost: ₹2 lakh per month
ROI: 2.7× to 5× return within the first month.
Conclusion
 Digitising Yard 1, Yard 2 and Bus Yard through Axestrack YMS will significantly reduce operational friction, bring complete traceability, speed up dispatch cycles, and deliver a strong and measurable monthly ROI.
YMS- itrack
Business Case – Yard Management System (YMS)
Ashok Leyland – Alwar RSO
(Designed to complement existing i-Track telematics)
1. Context
Every Ashok Leyland vehicle is already equipped with i-Track for outbound visibility.
 However, i-Track does not solve internal yard-level operations, because yard movements happen in tightly packed spaces where GPS cannot show which slot a vehicle is parked in.
Therefore, Axestrack’s YMS is designed as a micro-location and workflow layer, not a replacement for i-Track.
Slot-level accuracy will be captured using:
 • Driver’s mobile app (lat-long while parking in a line)
 • Optional integration with i-Track APIs (if needed)
This reduces complexity and avoids additional hardware while giving position-level traceability.
2. Current Challenges (Not Addressed by i-Track)
• Only line-level parking known; exact slot unknown
 • Retrieval across ~4,200 vehicles (Yard 1 + Yard 2 + Bus Yard) is manual
 • Vehicles frequently re-parked for rework/space but not digitally logged
 • VIN-based FIFO dispatch becomes slow because vehicles are hard to locate
 • No yard utilisation or free-space view
 • Buses move Production → RSO Yard → Bodywork Vendor → Bus Yard, without digital tracking
 • High dependency on driver/jockey memory → inconsistent operations
3. Proposed Solution – Axestrack Yard Management System (YMS)
A lightweight, low-infrastructure, mobile-first system using driver mobile app for lat-long capture.
Key Capabilities
• VIN-based digital check-in (QC / RSO / Bodywork / Bus Yard)
 • Slot-level parking captured via driver app (lat-long stamp per drop)
 • Real-time yard map showing free vs. occupied slots
 • Navigation-based retrieval guidance
 • Re-parking, rework and cross-yard movement history
 • FIFO dispatch automation
 • Driver/Jockey productivity analytics
 • Option to integrate i-Track for single-pane visibility
4. Commercials
Yard 1 + Yard 2: INR 2,00,000 per month total
 (Bus Yard can be added at small incremental cost)
5. ROI Case (Even With i-Track Already Deployed)
1. Retrieval Time Savings
• Today: 20–45 mins per vehicle
 • With YMS (lat-long based): 5–10 mins
 → 40–60 percent reduction
At 50–80 retrievals/day:
 → 20–30 labour hours saved daily
 = ₹3–4.5 lakh per month
2. Yard Utilisation Improvement
Accurate slot mapping across 4,200+ spaces
 → 10–20 percent better utilisation
 = ₹1–2 lakh per month
3. Eliminated Misplacements
No “lost” vehicles or incorrect re-parking
 = ₹0.5–1 lakh per month
4. Faster Dispatch → Faster Billing
Reducing dispatch cycle by even 1 day:
 → ₹1–3 lakh per month value
Total Monthly ROI
₹5.5 – ₹10.5 lakh per month
 vs.
 Cost: ₹2 lakh per month
ROI = 2.7× to 5× every month
6. Why YMS Is Still Required Even With i-Track
i-Track covers inter-city telematics, whereas YMS covers:
 • Slot-level micro-location
 • Yard occupancy
 • Re-parking/rework workflows
 • Retrieval navigation
 • Queuing and dispatch optimisation
 • Yard operations productivity
Together: i-Track + YMS = Full visibility from Production → Yard → Dispatch → Dealer

Ashok Leyland’s inbound logistics operations involve multiple fixed loading plants and unloading plants across India. While the current tracking platform provides en-route visibility of vehicles, it lacks plant-level integration for capturing gate-in and gate-out events. This creates:
Limited visibility of TAT: No accurate timestamps of vehicle entry and exit at plants.
Operational inefficiencies: Delay analysis is difficult without standardized TAT measurement.
Dependence on manual records: Increases scope of errors and exceptions.
Missed benchmarking opportunities: Plant-to-plant performance cannot be measured uniformly.
Business Need: To establish a standalone Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) solution that digitizes plant-level gate processes, integrates with existing Tracking Partner, and provides real-time monitoring of inbound vehicles TAT to enhance visibility, efficiency, and accountability across 8 Ashok Leyland plants

content:
Axestrack’s Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) platform will serve as a digital layer that complements existing vehicle tracking.
How the solution aligns with Ashok Leyland’s requirements:
Real-time Gate Digitization
Deploy RFID/FASTag/ANPR-enabled checkpoints at gate-in and gate-out.
Capture accurate entry/exit timestamps without manual intervention.
Integration with Grow System (API)
Vehicle master and inbound trip details fetched automatically.
Avoids duplication of effort and ensures a single version of truth.
Centralized TAT Computation
Automatic calculation of TAT from loading plant exit → unloading plant entry.
Plant-to-plant and stage-wise tracking enabled.
Visibility & Analytics Dashboards
Role-based access for Plant Operations, SCM, and Central Teams.
Daily/weekly/monthly TAT performance reports for benchmarking.
Readily Deployable Infrastructure
Tablets with SIMs at each gate to ensure connectivity.
RFID reader deployment for seamless automation.
Value Delivered:
Improved visibility and control of inbound movement.
Standardized TAT monitoring across all plants.
Accountability in operations with data-driven insights.
Scalable foundation for yard management, ERP integration, and AI-driven analytics (future phases).

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content:
The CPL solution will digitize and automate all inbound and outbound gate operations across Ashok Leyland’s 8 plants.
Automated Vehicle Identification:
Each vehicle is identified at entry/exit using RFID tags / FASTag ID / ANPR.
Tag data cross-verified against the vehicle master in CPL platform.
ULIP based verification for the RC/PUC/Insurance
Gate-In & Gate-Out Timestamping:
Automatic logging of date & time when vehicle enters or exits the plant gate.
No manual entry required, reducing human error.
TAT Computation at Gate Level:
Exit timestamp from loading plant → Entry timestamp at unloading plant.
Gate-wise performance metrics consolidated into overall Plant-to-Plant TAT.
Exception Handling:
Vehicles without valid RFID/FASTag/ANPR match flagged for manual verification.
Role-Based Gate Dashboard:
Security team: Gate event verification.
Plant operations: Queue monitoring, vehicle turnaround reports.
Central SCM team: Consolidated visibility across all plants.
MIS & Audit Trail:
Exception log (vehicles entered/exited without valid tags).
Audit trail for compliance and dispute resolution.

content:
Hardware & Integration:
Supply, installation, and commissioning of RFID readers/ ANPR Camera at gate entry/exit.
Integration with plant IT infrastructure (power, LAN/Wi-Fi).
Tablets with SIM Cards:
Tablets provided at each gate to record gate events.
Mobile network data plan included for seamless connectivity.
Software Integration:
CPL platform linked with Tracking Platform via API (maximum 3 APIs) for vehicle master/trip details.
Local gate device data synced in real-time with CPL cloud platform.
Data Flow & Processing:

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content:
Ashok Leyland requires a digital solution to capture accurate inbound Turnaround Time (TAT) between fixed loading and unloading plants. While the existing tracking partner provides en-route visibility, it lacks plant-level visibility at gate entry and exit points.
Axestrack’s Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) platform addresses this gap by deploying gate automation infrastructure (RFID/ANPR), integrating with the tracking partner (via API), and providing a centralized TAT visibility dashboard. The system will serve as a standalone, scalable layer that ensures real-time monitoring, analytics, and future integration with ERP and advanced yard management systems.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content:
The proposed CPL solution aligns with Ashok Leyland’s objectives in the following ways:
Accurate Gate Digitization – Automated capture of gate-in and gate-out timestamps using RFID/FASTag and ANPR cameras.
Integration with Tracking Partner – API connectors ensure inbound trip and vehicle details are seamlessly linked.
Centralized TAT Visibility – Plant-to-plant TAT computed automatically, visible in real-time dashboards.
Exception Handling – Hybrid approach (RFID + ANPR) reduces dependency on third-party data availability and minimizes manual interventions.
Scalability – Phase 1 lays a quick foundation, while Phase 2 builds advanced functionality (yard, ERP, AI-driven predictions).
Business Value Delivered – Standardized performance monitoring, improved accountability, operational efficiency, and readiness for digital transformation.

The unified, single-pane view across all platforms facilitates rapid and informed decision-making. This connected operating system is structured into three key layers: See, Understand & Learn, and Act.
The See layer aggregates data from multiple sources, including third-party GPS/Telematics systems, 3PL providers, TOS/ERP/TMS platforms, toll systems, government portals, OEM systems, third-party SIMs, and open APIs. This data is consolidated into a comprehensive data pool via a robust integration layer.
The Understand & Learn layer leverages advanced AI and machine learning capabilities to transform this data lake into a data pool, enabling deeper insights and meaningful analytics.
The Act layer presents this processed data through unified platforms, offering actionable analytics and reports. These insights empower the Managed Control Tower to make real-time interventions, ensuring efficient operations and improved outcomes.

content:
Yard operations currently run at scale but without position-level digital visibility (VIN tracked only by memory/line reference).
QC “Pass to Sales” → Yard → Re-park/Rework → Dispatch involves multiple movements where location/status is not reliably captured.
Bus bodywork vendor loop adds additional handoffs where traceability gaps compound.

filename: Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content:
FIFO-based dispatch becomes slow and error-prone when exact slot/position is unknown → search loops, delays, and re-handling.
Re-parking and space corrections break the “last known location” assumption, increasing operational risk as volumes grow.
No real-time free/occupied view limits planning, causes congestion, and forces reactive yard management.

filename: Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx

content:
Increased retrieval time and dispatch slippages (missed windows, lower dispatch reliability).
Higher operational effort (extra moves, avoidable driver/jockey time, overtime).
Greater risk and inconsistency due to dependence on individual memory (audit gaps, disputes, compliance exposure).

filename: Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content: The Yard Management Solution must manage all physical yard areas as a single logical Yard, providing one unified VIN inventory and search across the operation. It should digitize the complete VIN journey from QC “Pass to Sales” through yard parking, re-parking and rework movements, the bus bodywork vendor loop (gate-out/gate-in and return intake), and finally dispatch. The system must capture exact slot/position-level parking (with optional GPS/photo proof), record every movement with a full audit trail (who moved it, from–to location, timestamps, and reason), and enable FIFO-aligned dispatch readiness through picklists and guided retrieval with VIN validation. It must also provide real-time visibility of yard occupancy (free vs occupied slots), utilization and congestion, support exception handling and compliance controls, deliver operational dashboards and productivity analytics, and integrate as required with QC/ERP and dispatch planning systems (with optional linkage to outbound Track & Trace where applicable).

Edge + Cloud synergy ensuring instant gate response and central governance.
ISO 27001, IEC 62443, VAPT certifications ensuring enterprise-grade security.
Proven integration readiness with Infor LN, ULIP, VAHAN, and SARATHI APIs.
Ruggedized design and local fail-safe logic ensure 99.9% uptime even in network outages.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Bharat - Yard Solution\Yard Management Proposal Ashok LeyLand v2.docx
content:
Unified Yard Operations
Treat all physical areas as one logical Yard with internal zones for control, but single VIN inventory, single search, single dashboards.
QC-to-Yard Intake
Capture/receive QC “Pass to Sales” event and create VIN record for yard operations.
VIN-based intake with status updates (e.g., QC Cleared, In Yard, Hold).
Parking & Location Capture
Slot/position-level parking capture (Zone/Line/Lane/Slot/Position).
Optional proof capture: GPS coordinates and/or photo attachment.
Movement & Shunting Management
Record and control all movements with full traceability:
Park, Re-park, Move-to-Rework, Return-from-Rework, Space correction moves
Task assignment to drivers/jockeys with timestamps and completion validation.
Bus Bodywork Vendor Loop Tracking
Track movements: Yard → Vendor (Gate-out) → At Vendor → Return (Gate-in) → Yard re-intake and parking.
Retrieval & Dispatch Support
Dispatch picklist upload/integration (FIFO VIN list) and prioritization.
Guided retrieval (map/navigation view), VIN validation at pickup/staging/exit.
Dispatch staging status and vehicle release workflow.
Real-time Yard Visibility
Live yard map showing free vs occupied, utilization %, congestion view, search by VIN/status/zone.
Alerts for dwell threshold breaches, aging, pending tasks, exceptions.
Exceptions & Audit
Exception handling: VIN not found, wrong slot, blocked location, hold conditions.
Full movement history and audit trail by VIN/user/time.
Dashboards & Reporting
KPIs: retrieval TAT, dwell time, utilization, moves per VIN, jockey productivity, FIFO adherence, exceptions.
Exportable reports for shift/day/month reviews.

Optional interface to outbound visibility (e.g., Track & Trace milestone sync) if required.

Optional enablement hooks for GPS capture, camera/photo proof.
Optional readiness for ANPR/RFID/weighbridge integrations (only if explicitly included).

Audit logging for all key events and master changes.

Deployment on customer-approved infrastructure (cloud/on-prem/hybrid as per IT policy).
Standard environments: Dev/UAT/Prod with release management support.
Non-functional Requirements
Performance for expected VIN volume, concurrency, and reporting loads.
Data retention and backup/restore approach aligned to IT policies.

Requirements finalization, yard layout digitization/configuration, integration testing, UAT support.
Go-live support, SOPs, and user training.

content:
Ashok Leyland’s inbound logistics operations involve multiple fixed loading plants and unloading plants across India. While the current tracking platform provides en-route visibility of vehicles, it lacks plant-level integration for capturing gate-in and gate-out events. This creates:
Limited visibility of TAT: No accurate timestamps of vehicle entry and exit at plants.
Operational inefficiencies: Delay analysis is difficult without standardized TAT measurement.
Dependence on manual records: Increases scope of errors and exceptions.
Missed benchmarking opportunities: Plant-to-plant performance cannot be measured uniformly.
Business Need: To establish a standalone Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) solution that digitizes plant-level gate processes, integrates with existing Tracking Partner, and provides real-time monitoring of inbound vehicles TAT to enhance visibility, efficiency, and accountability across 8 Ashok Leyland plants

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
Axestrack’s Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) platform will serve as a digital layer that complements existing vehicle tracking.
How the solution aligns with Ashok Leyland’s requirements:
Real-time Gate Digitization
Deploy RFID/FASTag/ANPR-enabled checkpoints at gate-in and gate-out.
Capture accurate entry/exit timestamps without manual intervention.
Integration with Grow System (API)
Vehicle master and inbound trip details fetched automatically.
Avoids duplication of effort and ensures a single version of truth.
Centralized TAT Computation
Automatic calculation of TAT from loading plant exit → unloading plant entry.
Plant-to-plant and stage-wise tracking enabled.
Visibility & Analytics Dashboards
Role-based access for Plant Operations, SCM, and Central Teams.
Daily/weekly/monthly TAT performance reports for benchmarking.
Readily Deployable Infrastructure
Tablets with SIMs at each gate to ensure connectivity.
RFID reader deployment for seamless automation.
Value Delivered:
Improved visibility and control of inbound movement.
Standardized TAT monitoring across all plants.
Accountability in operations with data-driven insights.
Scalable foundation for yard management, ERP integration, and AI-driven analytics

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
Full hardware-software ownership (no third-party controllers or middleware).
Edge + Cloud synergy ensuring instant gate response and central governance.
ISO 27001, IEC 62443, VAPT certifications ensuring enterprise-grade security.
Proven integration readiness with Infor LN, ULIP, VAHAN, and SARATHI APIs.
Ruggedized design and local fail-safe logic ensure 99.9% uptime even in network outages.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
The CPL solution will digitize and automate all inbound and outbound gate operations across Ashok Leyland’s 8 plants.
Automated Vehicle Identification:
Each vehicle is identified at entry/exit using RFID tags / FASTag ID / ANPR.
Tag data cross-verified against the vehicle master in CPL platform.
ULIP based verification for the RC/PUC/Insurance
Gate-In & Gate-Out Timestamping:
Automatic logging of date & time when vehicle enters or exits the plant gate.
No manual entry required, reducing human error.
TAT Computation at Gate Level:
Exit timestamp from loading plant → Entry timestamp at unloading plant.
Gate-wise performance metrics consolidated into overall Plant-to-Plant TAT.
Exception Handling:
Vehicles without valid RFID/FASTag/ANPR match flagged for manual verification.
Role-Based Gate Dashboard:
Security team: Gate event verification.
Plant operations: Queue monitoring, vehicle turnaround reports.
Central SCM team: Consolidated visibility across all plants.
MIS & Audit Trail:
Exception log (vehicles entered/exited without valid tags).
Audit trail for compliance and dispute resolution.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
Hardware & Integration:
Supply, installation, and commissioning of RFID readers/ ANPR Camera at gate entry/exit.
Integration with plant IT infrastructure (power, LAN/Wi-Fi).
Tablets with SIM Cards:
Tablets provided at each gate to record gate events.
Mobile network data plan included for seamless connectivity.
Software Integration:
CPL platform linked with Tracking Platform via API (maximum 3 APIs) for vehicle master/trip details.
Local gate device data synced in real-time with CPL cloud platform.
Data Flow & Processing:
Gate device → CPL middleware → Cloud platform → Dashboard/MIS.
Automated alerts pushed via email/WhatsApp/IVR in case of anomalies.
Security & Access Control:
Role-based login access for gate operators, plant managers, and central teams.
Encrypted data transmission between gate hardware and CPL cloud.

content:

Civil infrastructure work (pole mounting, trenching, enclosures, LAN cabling, electrical) – to be facilitated by Ashok Leyland.

Non-inbound vehicle tracking – e.g., visitor or contractor vehicles unless tagged separately.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
Ashok Leyland requires a digital solution to capture accurate inbound Turnaround Time (TAT) between fixed loading and unloading plants. While the existing tracking partner provides en-route visibility, it lacks plant-level visibility at gate entry and exit points.
Axestrack’s Connected Plant Logistics (CPL) platform addresses this gap by deploying gate automation infrastructure (RFID/ANPR), integrating with the tracking partner (via API), and providing a centralized TAT visibility dashboard. The system will serve as a standalone, scalable layer that ensures real-time monitoring, analytics, and future integration with ERP and advanced yard management systems.

source_file: C:\Users\fx7jz\OneDrive\Desktop\Soldesk_chatbot-master\data\raw_proposals\Ashok Leyland\Inter plant movement\Proposal Ashok LeyLand v3.docx
content:
The proposed CPL solution aligns with Ashok Leyland’s objectives in the following ways:
Accurate Gate Digitization – Automated capture of gate-in and gate-out timestamps using RFID/FASTag and ANPR cameras.
Integration with Tracking Partner – API connectors ensure inbound trip and vehicle details are seamlessly linked.
Centralized TAT Visibility – Plant-to-plant TAT computed automatically, visible in real-time dashboards.
Exception Handling – Hybrid approach (RFID + ANPR) reduces dependency on third-party data availability and minimizes manual interventions.
Scalability – Phase 1 lays a quick foundation, while Phase 2 builds advanced functionality (yard, ERP, AI-driven predictions).
Business Value Delivered – Standardized performance monitoring, improved accountability, operational efficiency, and readiness for digital transformation.

content:
Axestrack has three layered approaches to Governance
Steering committee and value board: This layer will have the executive sponsor for the project with one-to-one mapping with the Ashok Leyland’s Sponsors level management. The team will meet monthly/quarterly to validate and monitor the project progress and take strategic decisions. This layer is the highest level of escalation,
Enagament & project management: This layer provides the project level monitoring and management. They will deal with the day-to-day operation issues and escalations. This layer will be responsible for the project success and excellence.
Project operations & transformation: This layer involves all the members of execution team including Technical SPOCs, functional SPOCs and SMEs. These will interact with the client project team on daily basis and work for the success of the project.

content:
Axestrack's unique advantage over competitors is our Multi Tracking mode, providing nearly 100% trip visibility irrespective of any failures
In the event that GPS fails, Axestrack's auto fail safe engine will automatically switch to Driver SIM. If, driver SIM also fails, the system will switch to Conductor SIM tracking, utilizing both the driver's and conductor's SIM cards. This ensures that the client maintains close to 100% visibility of their vehicle throughout the entire trip, providing continuous tracking and ensuring that the client never loses track of their vehicle.

Our flagship product, the Axestrack Operating System (commonly known as aOS), leverages cutting-edge AI technology to optimize supply chain operations. By providing real-time data, analytics, and insights, we empower businesses to make informed decisions, improve efficiency, and reduce cost.
Beyond telematics, we offer a comprehensive suite of digital solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. From seamless integration with ERP systems to advanced analytics and AI-driven insights, we empower businesses to build a robust digital thread that drives efficiency and growth. By digitizing information and processes, we help our clients stay ahead of the curve and achieve their long-term goals.

Axestrack with its suite of products (xSwift, Connected Plant Logistics, Managed Control Tower, Connected Plant Logistics, etc.) provides state-of-the-art tracking and visibility to shippers and fleet owners.
The unified, single-pane view across all platforms facilitates rapid and informed decision-making. This connected operating system is structured into three key layers: See, Understand & Learn, and Act.
The See layer aggregates data from multiple sources, including third-party GPS/Telematics systems, 3PL providers, ERP/TMS platforms, toll systems, government portals, OEM systems, third-party SIMs, and open APIs. This data is consolidated into a comprehensive data pool via a robust integration layer.
The Understand & Learn layer leverages advanced AI and machine learning capabilities to transform this data lake into a data pool, enabling deeper insights and meaningful analytics.
The Act layer presents this processed data through unified platforms, offering actionable analytics and reports. These insights empower the Managed Control Tower to make real-time interventions, ensuring efficient operations and improved outcomes.

Ather Energy currently manages its truck movement and material validation processes manually at the Security Gate, Vehicle Plant, and Battery Plant. The process begins with manual checks of vehicles at the security gate, where material and invoice validations are conducted. Following this, details are manually entered into JIRA, and a handwritten slip is created for the truck driver, indicating the destination. At junction points, security personnel manually guide drivers based on the slip, directing them to the designated docks for unloading. This reliance on manual processes leads to significant inefficiencies and operational challenges.
The diagram below illustrates the current As-is flow at Ather Energy
One of the primary issues is the high Truck TAT, with invoice validation at the gate often taking 2-3 hours. These delays cause truck drivers to park their vehicles in open areas and take extended breaks, creating further bottlenecks. Security personnel must then manually coordinate with the drivers to move trucks, increasing their workload. Additionally, there are frequent mismatches and miscommunications between dock operations teams and gate administration staff, complicating workflows and delaying material movement. The lack of a digital system also limits real-time visibility into truck and material tracking, while manual data entry into JIRA increases the risk of errors and consumes valuable time.

To address above challenges, Axestrack proposes implementing its "Connected Plant Logistics" (CPL) solution for Ather Energy. This solution automates the critical stages of truck-in, Unloading, and truck-out processes using advanced hardware such as RFID readers, PA systems, LED displays, and boom barriers. CPL will streamline the validation process, enabling faster and more accurate gate-in checks. By integrating directly with Ather’s ERP or Ariba systems, the solution eliminates the need for manual data entry into JIRA. All data points will be automatically captured and pushed to the ERP system, ensuring seamless operations and reducing the risk of human error.
The diagram below illustrates the To-be flow progression through various checkpoints for Ather Energy
The implementation of CPL will significantly reduce truck turnaround times, optimize material tracking, and enhance process transparency through a robust digital layer. Real-time tracking of trucks and materials will provide end-to-end visibility, while automated systems will improve coordination between dock operations and gate administration teams. By automating driver management and truck movement, CPL will ensure smoother operations, reducing delays caused by manual interventions. Furthermore, CPL will capture comprehensive data for audits and reports, enabling Ather Energy to gain actionable insights and maintain a detailed operational record.
Overall, the adoption of Axestrack's CPL solution will transform Ather Energy's logistics operations, enhancing efficiency, reducing costs, and providing a modern, automated framework for managing truck and material movements. This system will address current inefficiencies while future-proofing Ather’s logistics processes for sustained operational excellence.

CPL will address the core requirements of Ather Energy by providing the following functionalities:
Automation of Truck Processes:
Automating the truck-in, unloading, and truck-out processes through CPL.
Installation of hardware components such as RFID readers, boom barriers, PA systems, and LED displays at critical checkpoints.
System Integration:
Seamless integration of CPL with Ather Energy’s existing ERP system (or Ariba) to enable real-time data validation, data exchange, and material tracking.
Elimination of manual data entry into JIRA by automating the transfer of all captured data points directly to the ERP system.
Material Validation and Tracking:
Digital validation of truck details, invoices, and materials at the security gate.
Implementation of a real-time tracking system to monitor the movement of trucks and materials across different plants.
Driver Guidance and Coordination:
Automated communication of dock assignments to drivers via digital slips and LED displays.
Reduced dependency on manual guidance by security personnel, streamlining the coordination process.
Reporting and Auditing:
Automated capture of operational data for generating audit trails and detailed reports.
Provision of fortnightly reporting and actionable insights to support operational decision-making and continuous improvement.
Enhanced Security and Efficiency:
Implementation of automated boom barriers to regulate truck entry and exit.
Streamlining communication between gate administration and dock operations teams to resolve operational mismatches and improve coordination.
Reduction in Turnaround Time (TAT):
Minimizing truck processing time at the gate to ensure quicker entry and reducing bottlenecks.
Streamlining operations across various stages to enable faster movement and reduction in delays.
Operational Visibility:
Deployment of a centralized digital platform to monitor and track truck movements.
Enhancing transparency in material handling operations through real-time updates.

ATUL Limited operates large-scale chemical manufacturing units in Valsad and Ankleshwar, dispatching finished goods to domestic depots and ports (Hazira & Nhava Sheva) through market fleet transporters. With over 50 trips daily managed by 21 transporters, the company requires real-time visibility, trip compliance, and accountability across its outbound operations.
Their existing SIM-based and API-based tracking methods have proven ineffective due to dependency on external fleets, lack of SIM consent, and inconsistent data availability. To maintain operational excellence, safety compliance, and on-time deliveries, ATUL needs a reliable GPS-based plug-and-play solution with integrated reverse logistics and ERP/TMS connectivity.

Inability to track market fleet vehicles due to SIM and API consent issues.
Lack of unified trip visibility across multi-destination and port-bound routes.
Manual coordination with transporters causing delays and higher turnaround time (TAT).
Absence of structured reverse logistics leading to device loss and poor asset utilization.
Non-standardized proof of delivery and safety compliance monitoring.
These challenges result in limited visibility, data gaps, non-compliance, and higher operational costs, directly impacting supply chain efficiency and delivery reliability.

ATUL Limited operates manufacturing plants — Valsad being major chemical production hubs. Their logistics involve multi-destination and single-destination trips handled by 21 transporters, with outbound movements to Hazira and Nhava Sheva ports along with domestic depots.

Axestrack proposes a GPS-based Plug & Play Tracking Solution with integrated Reverse Logistics Management, ePOD, and MCT-based Safety Monitoring to provide ATUL full visibility, accountability, and compliance across its outbound and inbound operations.

Axestrack proposes a GPS-based Plug & Play Tracking Solution with integrated Reverse Logistics Management, ePOD, and MCT-based Safety Monitoring to provide ATUL full visibility, accountability, and compliance across its outbound and inbound operations.
