The Logistics Process Flow for ATUL Limited ensures secure and transparent movement of finished chemical products from plants to ports and domestic depots through Axestrack’s GPS-based Plug-and-Play solution integrated with the XSwift Platform.
The process begins with Loading, where goods are verified and loaded at the Valsad plant. In the GPS Installation Stage, a plug-and-play device is activated and mapped to the trip via the Axestrack app. Trip details including vehicle, driver, and route information are then created in the XSwift Platform, enabling live trip tracking and alert management.
During the In-Transit Phase, XSwift provides real-time visibility with alerts for route deviation, overspeeding, stoppages, and other exceptions. The Monitoring & Control Tower (MCT) team ensures safety and compliance by actively managing alerts and transporter coordination.
At the Destination, unloading and delivery are confirmed through ePOD, capturing photographic and digital proof. Post-delivery, the Reverse Logistics Process begins — GPS devices are retrieved either by trained drivers or managed directly by Axestrack. Returned devices undergo testing and inventory logging for redeployment in future trips.
This streamlined process ensures real-time visibility, safety compliance, and efficient device management, reducing operational delays and enhancing control across ATUL’s logistics network.

Trip Management:

ATUL Limited operates large-scale chemical manufacturing units in Valsad and Ankleshwar, dispatching finished goods to domestic depots and ports (Hazira & Nhava Sheva) through market fleet transporters. With over 50 trips daily managed by 21 transporters, the company requires real-time visibility, trip compliance, and accountability across its outbound operations.
Their existing SIM-based and API-based tracking methods have proven ineffective due to dependency on external fleets, lack of SIM consent, and inconsistent data availability. To maintain operational excellence, safety compliance, and on-time deliveries, ATUL needs a reliable GPS-based plug-and-play solution with integrated reverse logistics and ERP/TMS connectivity.

  Inability to track market fleet vehicles due to SIM and API consent issues.
  Lack of unified trip visibility across multi-destination and port-bound routes.
  Manual coordination with transporters causing delays and higher turnaround time (TAT).
  Absence of structured reverse logistics leading to device loss and poor asset utilization.
  Non-standardized proof of delivery and safety compliance monitoring.
These challenges result in limited visibility, data gaps, non-compliance, and higher operational costs, directly impacting supply chain efficiency and delivery reliability.

The Logistics Process Flow for ATUL Limited ensures secure and transparent movement of finished chemical products from plants to ports and domestic depots through Axestrack’s GPS-based Plug-and-Play solution integrated with the XSwift Platform.
The process begins with Loading, where goods are verified and loaded at the Valsad and Ankleshwar plants. In the GPS Installation Stage, a plug-and-play device is activated and mapped to the trip via the Axestrack app. Trip details including vehicle, driver, and route information are then created in the XSwift Platform, enabling live trip tracking and alert management.
During the In-Transit Phase, XSwift provides real-time visibility with alerts for route deviation, overspeeding, stoppages, and other exceptions. The Monitoring & Control Tower (MCT) team ensures safety and compliance by actively managing alerts and transporter coordination.
At the Destination, unloading and delivery are confirmed through ePOD, capturing photographic and digital proof. Post-delivery, the Reverse Logistics Process begins — GPS devices are retrieved either by trained drivers or managed directly by Axestrack. Returned devices undergo testing and inventory logging for redeployment in future trips.
This streamlined process ensures real-time visibility, safety compliance, and efficient device management, reducing operational delays and enhancing control across ATUL’s logistics network.
